Head to our website to get in touch with our team and learn more about the long-term investments being made in Columbia County.', 'Clark County Board Tackles Solar Farm Opposition, Approves Homeschool Sanctuary Resolution Marshall, Ill. – June 23, 2025 – The Clark County Board meeting on June 20th, 2025, saw passionate community input on two pressing issues: the controversial Moonshine and Six Summit Solar projects and a resolution to designate Clark County as a homeschool sanctuary county. Residents voiced concerns about farmland loss, property values, and mental health impacts from the solar farms, while homeschool advocates pushed for protections against state overreach. The board navigated legal constraints and community sentiment, tabling the Moonshine Solar decision and approving the homeschool resolution. Solar Farm Projects Face Strong Community Opposition The proposed Moonshine Solar project, spanning 1,665 acres, drew significant resistance from residents, who cited its impact on farmland, livelihoods, and property values. Dusty Hickox, a southwest Clark County resident living near the project, expressed frustration, stating, “It’s going to affect that area. We live there to farm. You can’t farm solar panels.” Hickox, who declined to lease his land for solar panels, emphasized the permanent loss of farmland, noting, “I don’t care what they say, 35 years from now, you’re not going to farm it.” He disputed claims that property values would rise, asserting, “There ain’t a person in this room or probably anywhere else in the world that’s going to believe that.” Jennifer Hickox, representing local farm families, described the personal toll, saying, “My husband and several other farm families are losing ground… That directly impacts our families. We have six children.” She highlighted the loss of income for feeding her children and funding college, adding, “Our land and home, that was my insurance policy… If something happens to him, I will need the full value to raise our children.” Jennifer also criticized the project’s impact on wildlife and dismissed assurances about property values, stating, “If you give energy to your property, we’ll lose value. You can’t promise us safety.” Edward Talbert, whose property is surrounded by the Moonshine project, raised legal concerns, arguing that the June 10, 2025, meeting violated the Illinois Open Meetings Act (OMA) due to inadequate notice. “The meeting held on June 10th, 2025 did not have the required notices for OMA. In my opinion, the review of special permit application… was illegal, improper, and invalid,” Talbert said. He reported the violation to the Illinois Attorney General and noted deficiencies in the application, including missing legal descriptions of non-participating residences. Talbert also shared the project’s impact on his mental health, stating, “I’m already dealing with depression and anxiety due to the destruction of my retirement plans, personal goals, and the fact that I will be forced to accept the change to my views of nature.” Another resident sharing a boundary with the Moonshine project land, read a letter from project representative Paul Iver, which stated, “However, this is your home first, and we wouldn’t want to impede or disrupt that. Marshall might not be the place for us, and that’s okay. Because honestly, in order to succeed, we definitely need the community behind us. And if you’re not into it, that’s fine.” The neighbor noted widespread community opposition and offered to share the letter for transparency. The Six Summit Solar project faced similar concerns about inadequate public notice, though it met some publication requirements by appearing in the Toledo paper. However, the board admitted failing to post the June 10 meeting agenda online 48 hours in advance, affecting both projects’ compliance with OMA. To address this, the board tabled the Moonshine Solar vote until a special meeting on July 7, 2025, at 6 p.m. at the extension office. The board acknowledged state laws limiting their authority to reject solar projects, with legal advice indicating they cannot vote “no” unless applications fail to meet county or state requirements. “He said if it meets everything in our ordinance, it meets everything in the state law; he said you cannot vote no,” a board member noted. Concerns about decommissioning bonds were raised, with estimates in the seven-to-eight-figure range to cover cleanup costs, adjusted every five years. The board expressed frustration with state-driven green energy policies, citing laws that override local zoning. “They passed a law where that doesn’t work anymore… They took those every time that we think we could pass something; they passed something in Springfield to take it away from us,” one member said. Homeschool Sanctuary Resolution Passes Unanimously The board also addressed a resolution to designate Clark County as a homeschool sanctuary county, led by Cora Weber of the International Alliance for Freedom in Home Education. Weber urged the board to protect homeschooling families from potential state overreach, particularly opposing HB 2827, a bill introduced in Springfield that would impose strict regulations. “It provided for things that would be so egregious where you would have to essentially waive your first, fourth, fifth, and tenth amendment protections just so you could home educate your child,” Weber explained. Despite 125,000 witness slips opposing the bill—a record for Illinois—it remains a threat, as Weber noted, “They said that they will be bringing it back in the next session.” The resolution, already adopted by 10% of Illinois counties like Effingham and Coles, serves as a symbolic stand against restrictive legislation. “It’s really a statement to the legislature that you have our backs if a bad bill is passed,” Weber said. The board clarified that the resolution cannot change state law but signals support for parental rights. After discussion, including concerns about truancy officer workloads, the board unanimously approved the resolution with a motion and vote: “All in favor say ‘aye.’ Aye.” Jennifer, who also spoke against the solar farm, reinforced the homeschooling perspective, stating, “We don’t even use the public school system, and you couldn’t pay us to.” She criticized local schools, noting, “In Casey, 73% of students in one grade can’t read aloud. That’s not a money issue. That’s a leadership issue,” underscoring her family’s commitment to homeschooling. Looking Ahead The Clark County Board’s decisions reflect the tension between state mandates and local priorities. The tabling of the Moonshine Solar vote offers residents more time to voice concerns, while the homeschool sanctuary resolution affirms support for parental choice. As the July 7 meeting approaches, the community remains divided on balancing green energy with preserving Clark County’s agricultural heritage. #Illinois #solarenergy #clarkcountyillinois #homeschool #homeschooling #solarpower']

Representative_Docs: ['"America needs an economic reset, and Trump is doing it. Is this the right reset? I don’t know. All economists believe that tariffs are bad for the economy. Everyone who attended a college economics or political science course was taught the danger of tariffs. So, if these tariffs work, we will have to burn every economic textbook in the world. But if they don’t, it’ll be a mistake to revert to the status quo ante. We will have to find another solution because the economic numbers have been average to bad for a long time. First, of course, is our national debt problem. We are paying more interest on the debt than on defense. For the first time in U.S. history, U.S. federal debt ($36 trillion) is larger than the GDP ($30 trillion). In other words, we owe 20% more than we produce every year. This is not sustainable. As our debt grows, we will have to increase taxes and abruptly cut benefits when interest on our debt goes up. And that will destroy the economy and create a lot of elder poverty like the 1930s. This is a two-part problem. The obvious issue is that we spend too much. But the other problem is that we take too little revenue because the economy is not growing enough. Real economic growth—that is, growth adjusted for inflation—has consistently been 3% or lower. In the 1990s, it used to be over 4%. Low growth also means fewer jobs and a slow increase in wealth and income. Now, people will tell you that the unemployment numbers are very low. Which is true on a spreadsheet, however two points: First, part of that was Biden/Harris putting government hiring on steroids. More Government jobs were brought into our economy during Biden\'s economic stewardship. Second, a better indicator is the labor force participation rate. It has been steadily declining. At the turn of the century, it peaked at 67.5%. Now it’s 62.5%. A 5% difference is a lot. It means that between 8 to 9 million people have become so discouraged by the economy that they are not even looking for jobs anymore. This is costing America more than half a trillion dollars in wealth and between $100 to $200 billion in tax revenue every year. Another problem is the gap in growth. Since the 1980s, incomes, adjusted for inflation, have grown for everyone. But for low-paying jobs, that growth has been 17%. The more you earn, the faster your income grows. The growth rate is 21% for the lower-middle class and 24% for the upper-middle class. And high-wage earners’ incomes have grown 46%. Everybody is getting richer, but not at the same pace, not even close. And it gets worse. The biggest problem is probably upward mobility. Once upon a time in America, you could be anyone and become anyone. This is no longer the case. It’s very difficult to climb the income ladder once you enter middle age. 39% of people in their 30s are in the same economic class as 10 years ago. 49% of people in the 40s are in the same class as 10 years ago. And 60% of people in their 50s are in the same class as 10 years ago. If you are a low-wage or lower-middle-class worker in your 40s, there’s only a 50% chance that you’ll enter the middle class. None of these numbers look good. We need an economic reset. Let’s see if the tariffs work (give them short leash). If they don’t, we will have to think of something else. But we cannot continue the ways of the past two decades, that the lower you go on the income bracket, the less likely it is that your life gets better. We used to be a country where immigrants would come here and become middle-class with comfortable lives. Forget about immigrants. Today, even Americans cannot move up the ladder because our economy is becoming stagnant like Europe." https://breakingbattlegrounds.substack.com/p/will-trumps-tariffs-work', "Democrats are the party of TAXES. All democrats care about is taking more of your hard earned money so they can enrich themselves. The federal Government collects taxes to give citizens the illusion that these taxes are funding the Govenrment. This is all smoke and mirrors. Fact is, the federal government largely funds its bureaucracy by printing more and more money. ￼ 1. **Federal Income Tax** (1913): - Democrats were instrumental in the passage of the 16th Amendment, which allowed for a federal income tax. Initially set at a low rate, it was meant to fund government operations post-World War I. 2. **Corporate Income Tax** (1894): - Although initially struck down by the Supreme Court, Democrats were among those who pushed for its establishment. It was later implemented in 1909 under Republican President William Howard Taft but with significant Democratic support. 3. **Inheritance (Estate) Tax** (1916): - Democrats played a key role in establishing this tax as a means of wealth redistribution, aiming to reduce large inheritances. 4. **Social Security Tax** (1935): - Part of Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal, Democrats established Social Security taxes to fund the social welfare program. 5. **Increased Tax Rates During WWII**: - FDR's administration significantly increased tax rates, including the highest marginal tax rate reaching 94% in 1944 and 1945, to fund war efforts. 6. **Post-WWII High Income Tax Rates**: - Democrats supported maintaining high marginal tax rates, with the top rate at 91% from 1944 through 1963, to address budget deficits and fund public programs. 7. **Medicare and Medicaid Taxes** (1965): - Under President Lyndon B. Johnson, Democrats helped establish additional payroll taxes to fund Medicare and Medicaid. 8. **Capital Gains Tax** (1921): - While established earlier, Democrats have historically pushed for higher or maintaining capital gains taxes, arguing for tax fairness. 9. **Alternative Minimum Tax (AMT)**: - Democrats have defended and fought to keep this tax, designed to ensure high-income earners pay a minimum amount of tax. 10. **Higher Corporate Taxes**: - Democrats have advocated for increasing corporate tax rates, especially in recent decades, to fund government programs and reduce income inequality. 11. **Tax on High Incomes**: - Various proposals under Democratic leadership aimed at raising taxes on incomes over certain thresholds, like the suggestion by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez to raise the top rate to 70% for incomes over $10 million. 12. **Carbon Tax** (In Some States): - While not a federal initiative, Democrats in states like Washington have supported carbon taxes to combat climate change. 13. **Payroll Tax Increases** (Various): - Democrats have supported or proposed increases in payroll taxes for funding social programs, like the Affordable Care Act's measures. 14. **Capital Gains as Ordinary Income**: - Proposals to tax capital gains at the same rate as ordinary income have been pushed by Democrats to increase tax revenue from the wealthy. 15. **Inheritance Tax Rate Increases**: - Efforts to increase or maintain estate tax rates have been a recurring theme in Democratic tax policy to address wealth inequality. 16. **Luxury Taxes**: - In the past, Democrats have supported taxes on luxury goods to generate revenue and promote economic equality.", 'TLDR Version: "A decrease in rates can result in lower borrowing costs, boosted consumer spending, and enhanced job market prospects—an essential formula for fostering economic growth." How Does the Fed\'s Fund Rate Change Affect the Working Class? The Federal Reserve\'s adjustments to the federal funds rate can have significant implications for the U.S. economy and, in particular, for the working class. While discussions often focus on rate hikes, the effects of a decrease in the federal funds rate can be equally impactful. Understanding this relationship is vital for recognizing how monetary policy shapes the financial realities of everyday Americans. What Is the Federal Funds Rate? The federal funds rate is the interest rate at which banks lend reserves to each other overnight. Changes in this rate directly influence the broader economic landscape, affecting borrowing costs, consumer spending, and overall financial stability. When the Fed lowers the rate, it aims to stimulate economic activity, particularly during periods of sluggish growth or rising unemployment. The Influence of Lower Borrowing Costs One of the most immediate effects of a decreased federal funds rate is the reduction in borrowing costs. For working-class families, this translates to lower interest rates on mortgages, auto loans, and credit cards. As borrowing becomes cheaper, many families find it easier to make significant purchases, stimulating their household economies and encouraging spending. Lower interest rates often lead to increased consumer confidence, allowing working-class families to invest in homes, cars, and other essential goods and services. Housing Market Benefits A decrease in the federal funds rate can also provide a significant boost to the housing market. Lower mortgage rates make homebuying more affordable for many in the working class. This surge in demand for housing can stimulate construction, benefitting those in trade jobs and related industries. Homeownership is a crucial financial stepping stone for many families, and a favorable interest rate environment can help more working-class individuals achieve this goal. Job Market Momentum When the Fed cuts rates, it can unleash positive momentum in the job market. Lower borrowing costs can incentivize businesses to invest in expansion and hiring, leading to job creation. This can be particularly valuable for working-class individuals, as more job opportunities often lead to increased wages and better working conditions. The prospect of increased employment can help reduce unemployment rates and elevate living standards for countless Americans. Addressing Cost of Living Concerns While lower interest rates generally stimulate consumer spending and economic growth, they can also play a role in addressing the cost of living for working-class families. Cheaper loans mean lower monthly payments on existing debts, allowing families to allocate more of their income to essential expenses like food, healthcare, and education. This shift can help ease the financial burden many working-class households face in an ever-increasing cost environment. The Role of Inflation It\'s important to note that while lower interest rates can create benefits, they must also be balanced against the risk of inflation. If the economy grows too quickly, inflation may rise, potentially negating the advantages that lower rates provide. The challenge for the Federal Reserve is to navigate this delicate balance, ensuring that rate cuts lead to sustainable growth without igniting unwanted price increases. Enhancements in Savings and Investment For those working-class individuals able to save, a decreased federal funds rate can also lead to improved opportunities for investment. Although low-interest rates mean lower returns on savings accounts, they can also encourage investment in the stock market, real estate, and small businesses. While not all working-class families have the luxury of saving or investing, for those who do, a favorable economic environment can open doors to wealth accumulation. Conclusion In summary, the Federal Reserve\'s decisions regarding the federal funds rate play a powerful role in shaping the economic landscape for the working class. A decrease in rates can result in lower borrowing costs, boosted consumer spending, and enhanced job market prospects—an essential formula for fostering economic growth. As the Fed continues to adjust its monetary policy, the implications for working-class families will remain a crucial component of the broader economic narrative. By focusing on these changes, policymakers can better understand the priorities and challenges faced by this vital segment of society.']

Name: 19_offshore drilling_drilling_earthjustice_drill
Representative_Docs: ['BREAKING: Earthjustice is suing to block Trump’s illegal ocean drilling order. \u200b\u200b Today, we filed the first major environmental lawsuit against the new Trump administration. We’re challenging an illegal order by President Trump to remove protections for vulnerable areas of the ocean from future oil-and-gas leasing. \u200b\u200b President Biden protected millions of acres of public waters along every U.S. coast, where 40% of Americans reside, by invoking his authority under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act. But under the same law, the president cannot revoke the protections of prior presidents.\u200b\u200b “We defeated Trump the first time he tried to roll back protections and sacrifice more of our waters to the oil industry. We’re bringing this abuse of the law to the courts again.” -Earthjustice managing attorney for oceans Steve Mashuda.\u200b \u200b', 'An interesting move at the end of the current administration that will bring conflicts over energy policy and offshore drilling to the forefront of U.S. politics quickly. The "eastern Gulf of Mexico" discussed in this article, among other areas that were just made off-limits to drilling, is Florida\'s Gulf. Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, and Alabama, the other Gulf states, already have offshore oil and gas operations that produce millions of barrels of oil daily. The hurricane-prone Gulf region also contains 48 percent of U.S. oil refining capacity. Though Florida voters passed a permanent constitutional ban on offshore drilling in 2018, that ban only covers state waters - nine miles out in the Gulf. The rest are federal waters and open to leasing by the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (an agency under the Department of the Interior). Given the proximity to the massive oil and gas infrastructure already in place in the Gulf and the expectation that a lot of oil and gas are present, the industry has long fought for access. Before the previous moratorium on eastern Gulf drilling expired in 2022 (from GOMESA – the Gulf of Mexico Energy and Security Act), President Trump extended the ban to 2032 under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA). Seemed like forever at the time, but now that\'s just 7 years away. It will come up quickly. We\'ve worked on this issue for years and remain convinced that the Florida Gulf Coast - with its mangroves, seagrass beds, beaches, tourist-based economy, and endless varieties of marine life is NOT a suitable place to drill. Though the intent to drill safely is there, accidents have always been a part of this industry. We\'re happy that Biden signed this ban even if the Trump administration and the current U.S. Congress vow to eventually overturn it. We also support the Florida Coastal Protection Act introduced by some Florida legislators which aims to permanently ban oil drilling off Florida’s coast. More to come. https://www.foxnews.com/politics/trump-plans-reverse-bidens-ban-oil-gas-drilling-us-coast', "In his inauguration speech yesterday, President Trump once again vowed to “Drill Baby Drill.” But that point of view will likely not be embraced by many - even in his own party. When Florida voters approved a constitutional amendment to ban offshore oil and gas drilling in state waters in 2018, the measure passed with nearly 70 percent of the vote. Environmental concerns in a state where coastal and marine tourism is a massive part of the economy were at the center of the opposition. He has also stressed making America “energy dominant” – but as the data below (second link) from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) shows, the U.S. has been the #1 producer of oil in the world since 2018. We also became the #1 producer of natural gas in 2018. We're #4 in coal production behind China, India, and Indonesia. In this case, politics and reality may not mesh. And even in Alaska, where the state government backs opening more of Alaska to oil drilling, a recent auction for drilling the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge (ANWR) received no bids at all from would-be drillers. U.S. oil reserves (not nearly the largest in the world - we're 9th behind Venezuela, Saudi Arabia, Iran, Iraq, Canada and other countries) are now much more accessible due to the fracking revolution of the 2000s. We also have a massive network of refineries, pipelines, and offshore oil infrastructure, contributing to the current U.S. dominance in oil and gas production. As far as energy prices going down if we just drill more, that's not likely. Oil is a worldwide commodity and the price per barrel is set on the world market ($77.24 as of today). It's not a local or even national market and is not going to be impacted by drilling in places like the Big Cypress National Preserve or Florida's Gulf. According to the EIA, the world produces 103 million barrels per day of oil. That's just a bit less than Florida has produced in its entire history of drilling in two oil fields in Southwest Florida and the Western Panhandle (a total of 120 million barrels from 1943 to the present). Seems like an excellent time to pump resources into non-fossil fuel technology and achieve dominance in that area (much better for our planet and provides a long-term, renewable supply). With a concentrated push, there's no telling what we could come up with. Also, please note, this page is managed by a 501c3 non-profit. We are required by law to be non-partisan. Energy policy (the focus of this post) is an incredibly important topic with regards to the environment and should get lots of (civil) debate. Please keep comments on-topic and leave the partisan politics out. https://rollcall.com/2025/01/14/drill-maybe-drill-trumps-plans-may-hit-political-reality/ https://www.eia.gov/todayinenergy/detail.php?id=61545&fbclid=IwY2xjawH8J3JleHRuA2FlbQIxMAABHbV04q8AIJMMiVDnNJY5YgBbRSGsuXhezceFohy6yP0uCvPb6wxPAfox9Q_aem_EF5aFJMyWDIOzn7y0X819A"]

Representative_Docs: ['How Internal Combustion Engines Are Destroying American Industry As the world shifts toward electric vehicles (EVs), the U.S. risks falling behind in one of the most significant industrial transformations of the century. While countries like China, Germany, and South Korea are investing heavily in high-tech EV manufacturing, the U.S. clings to outdated internal combustion engine (ICE) technologies. This isn’t just an environmental issue—it’s an economic and national security crisis in the making. Here’s why: 1. Loss of Global Leadership: The U.S. once led the auto industry, but failing to embrace EVs on a large scale allows other nations to dominate high-tech manufacturing. China has already surpassed the U.S. in solar panel production, and now they’re leading in EVs and battery innovation. Falling behind in EV manufacturing could mean a permanent loss of leadership in the global economy. 2. Brain Drain: High-tech industries attract the best and brightest engineers, scientists, and innovators. If other countries outpace the U.S. in EV and battery production, our talent will go abroad to work on cutting-edge technologies, leaving America behind in all advanced manufacturing sectors. 3. Ripple Effect on Manufacturing: Advanced manufacturing isn’t limited to EVs. Falling behind in this sector will undermine the entire industrial base, from robotics to renewable energy technologies. If we lose our edge here, it will cascade into every facet of manufacturing, weakening the backbone of our economy. 4. National Security Risk: A strong manufacturing base isn’t just about jobs—it’s about defense. Wars aren’t won with outdated factories; they’re won with advanced technology and a skilled workforce. If the U.S. loses its manufacturing edge, we’ll lack the capacity to produce the tools and technology necessary to defend ourselves in a crisis. 5. Economic Drain: The continued reliance on ICE vehicles means sending billions of dollars overseas to buy oil, often from countries that don’t align with our values. That’s money we could be investing in American jobs, infrastructure, and innovation. 6. Environmental and Public Health Costs: ICE vehicles contribute to air pollution and climate change, which directly impact public health and drive up healthcare costs. By delaying the transition to EVs, we’re burdening future generations with preventable economic and environmental damage. America’s future depends on its ability to adapt and lead. Embracing EVs and renewable energy isn’t just about saving the planet—it’s about saving American industry, protecting jobs, and ensuring our national security. The choice is clear: invest in the future or be left behind in the past. Let’s demand that our leaders, automakers, and industries commit to innovation before it’s too late. The stakes couldn’t be higher. Stay charged with the latest EV tips, news, and insights—like, follow, and share to help others discover this post and join the journey to a greener future!', 'What’s in the budget bill for EV drivers? ❌ EV tax credits would disappear, making it harder for everyone to experience the benefits of driving electric ❌ A new annual tax of up to $500 per EV ❌ Higher energy costs for families ❌ Lost jobs This affects millions of us. Then call daily until they vote. #SaveEVIncentives #TakeAction #EVcommunity', 'Chinese EVs help drive Africa\'s green transition From Kenya to South Africa, Chinese electric vehicle manufacturers are ramping up investment in Africa, a move that experts said will accelerate the continent\'s shift toward clean mobility and sustainable development. Chinese EV giant BYD recently announced plans to nearly triple its dealership network in South Africa by next year, as it seeks to expand its market share in the country, according to Steve Chang, general manager of BYD South Africa. Chang said the company plans to increase the number of its dealerships in South Africa to 20 by the end of this year and aims to further expand its dealerships to 30 to 35 by the end of next year. The company is currently implementing its hybrid and electric dual power train strategy, selling six models in the local market. Beyond South Africa, BYD is also deepening its presence in the East Africa region. The Chinese carmaker is collaborating with BasiGo, a provider of electric bus solutions in sub-Saharan Africa based in Nairobi, the capital of Kenya, to merge Chinese EV expertise with Kenyan assembly, financing and renewable infrastructure. BAIC, another Chinese automaker, is partnering with Alkan Auto, a subsidiary of Egyptian International Motors Group, to establish a new factory in Egypt that is expected to become operational by the end of this year. The new EV assembly plant aligns with BAIC\'s ambitious goal of producing 50,000 units annually within five years, targeting markets across the African continent. "China is a key supplier of EVs to Africa, as Chinese EV brands are becoming increasingly popular among African consumers," said Dennis Wakaba, a consultant at the Electric Mobility Association of Kenya. Wakaba emphasized that harnessing Chinese EV technology is vital for Kenya and the broader African continent in advancing the transition to renewable energy in e-mobility. According to a report by the International Energy Agency, the transportation sector accounts for roughly 24 percent of Africa\'s carbon dioxide emissions from fuel combustion. Reducing emissions The United Nations Environment Programme estimated that by decarbonizing transportation and energy systems, the continent could prevent 200,000 deaths annually by 2030 — a figure that could rise to 880,000 by 2063 — while also reducing CO2 emissions by 55 percent and methane emissions by 74 percent. It is therefore essential to build the EV industry locally, as African countries are among the hardest hit by climate change, Wakaba said. "EVs can significantly reduce local emissions in areas where they\'re used, especially given that fossil fuel-based power generation is one of the highest sources of emissions," said Stephen Dyer, partner and managing director at AlixPartners, a global business advisory firm. "Africa presents a long-term growth opportunity for the automotive industry, despite its currently small market size. Given China\'s long-standing presence on the continent through various companies and products, it is well-positioned to play a significant role in the region\'s automotive market expansion," Dyer said. He noted that Chinese battery suppliers now lead the global lithium-ion automotive battery market, thanks to their scale and experience. At the same time, Chinese automakers are making rapid strides in autonomous driving and smart vehicle software, with more than half of their engineering workforce dedicated to software development. "Chinese EV brands are particularly strong in smart vehicle features, including intelligent cockpits and advanced driver-assistance systems, which cover lower levels of autonomous driving. They also offer appealing designs and solid mechanical performance at relatively affordable prices, which helps attract African consumers," Dyer said. Electric car sales in Africa more than doubled in 2024, reaching around 11,000 units, yet they still accounted for less than 1 percent of the continent\'s total vehicle market, according to the IEA\'s "Global EV Outlook 2025" report. Due to the absence of government incentives that could help narrow the price gap between EVs and more affordable gasoline-powered vehicles, as well as limited availability of charging infrastructure, EV sales in Africa are expected to grow more slowly in the medium term, Dyer said. Chinese automakers currently dominate global EV production and sales, which positions them better to play a pivotal role in driving EV adoption in emerging markets including Africa, he said. With adequate support from local governments for charging infrastructure and EV incentives, Chinese EV manufacturers could play a significant role in helping African nations meet their decarbonization targets, he added. (via China Daily)']

Name: 22_ocean_coral_marine_absorbing
Representative_Docs: ['Day for Biodiversity 🌊 Biodiversity is the foundation of life on Earth, and nowhere is that more evident than in our oceans. From microscopic plankton to apex predators like sharks, marine ecosystems are complex, delicate, and essential to the health of our planet. At Marine Dynamics, we’re privileged to work in one of the world’s most biodiverse marine environments, the waters off Gansbaai, South Africa. But this biodiversity is under increasing threat from climate change, overfishing, pollution, and habitat loss. Why ocean biodiversity matters: Oceans produce over 50% of the oxygen we breathe Marine species regulate climate, support food security, and drive coastal economies Sharks play a key role in maintaining the balance of marine ecosystems as apex predators Through shark cage diving, we offer more than just a close encounter, we provide education, inspiration, and funding for critical conservation work. Our guests leave with a deeper understanding of sharks and their role in ocean health, and often become passionate advocates for marine protection. By supporting ethical eco tourism, you’re helping us fund marine research, rescue efforts, and environmental education that directly contributes to protecting this incredible biodiversity. #BiodiversityDay #InternationalDayForBiologicalDiversity #MarineDynamics #ProtectOurOceans #SharkConservation #EthicalEcotourism #Gansbaai #SharkCageDivingWithPurpose #SaveOurSeas', '"The ocean is our planet\'s lifeline, and the fight to protect it has never been more urgent. Name: 23_energy storage_storage_bess_battery
Representative_Docs: ['The recent 100+ degree heat wave prompted emergency alerts urging New Yorkers to reduce electricity and AC use — a clear reminder that our aging grid is already under strain and will only face greater pressure as severe weather and climate change intensify. In their recent op-ed, Costa Constantinides and Tom Grech explain why battery energy storage systems (BESS) are essential to modernizing NYC’s power system: ✅ Replace peaker plants that pollute our air ✅ Create local jobs and economic opportunity ✅ Support clean, reliable energy ✅ Meet the highest safety standards It’s time to move past misinformation and invest in solutions that protect our neighborhoods, our children and our climate future. Read the full op-ed here: https://www.amny.com/opinion/support-battery-energy-storage-systems-new-york-city/', 'What exactly is a Battery Energy Storage System (BESS)? We’ve gotten some requests to elaborate on exactly what BESS do and why they are needed. A battery energy storage system charges by taking AC power from the grid (or a co-located generation facility) and converting it to DC power to store in batteries. The system will automatically stop charging once the battery is at full charge. When there is an energy need on the grid, the system discharges energy back to the grid by converting the energy from DC back into AC. The American Clean Power Association says it best, “Energy storage fundamentally improves the way we generate, deliver, and consume electricity. Energy storage helps during emergencies like power outages from storms, equipment failures, or accidents. But the game-changing nature of energy storage is its ability to balance power supply and demand instantaneously – within milliseconds – which makes power networks more resilient, efficient, and cleaner than ever before.” (http://energystorageassociationarchive.org/resources/thought-leadership/faqs/) These facilities are sited to maximize benefits to the grid and to customers. They are typically closer to the electrical load and/or transmission lines to ensure they are able to do what they need to do!', 'The American Clean Power Association has recently published an outstanding Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) safety framework to help encourage the use of the U.S.’s most rigorous safety standards when building battery storage projects. “At its core, energy storage facilities are critical infrastructure designed to protect people from power outages,” said ACP VP of Energy Storage Noah Roberts. “Every community across the country should have confidence that the battery storage facilities keeping their lights on and utility bills affordable adhere to the most rigorous safety requirements. This is why we have released the battery storage industry’s comprehensive, actionable blueprint for safety.” Setauket Energy Storage is proud to follow these high standards and is committed to safety as the top priority in construction, operation, and eventual decommission. Read more here: https://solarbuildermag.com/batteries/in-wake-of-moss-landing-fire-acp-publishes-bess-safety-blueprint/']

Name: 24_virginia_virginia clean_youngkin_energy virginia
Representation: ['virginia', 'virginia clean', 'youngkin', 'energy virginia', 'economy act', 'clean economy', 'virginians', 'virginia gov', 'https energy', 'virginia energy']
Representative_Docs: ["Having already driven major emissions reductions through the transition from coal to cleaner-burning natural gas, Virginia can maintain this momentum with nuclear power working alongside renewables and natural gas to create a balanced grid that is reliable, affordable, and increasingly clean. Learn more about Virginia's energy future at https://energy.virginia.gov/va-energy-plan.shtml", 'To register: https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_4cZh-B_MRzi3QuFVbQLesQ Affordable access to clean energy is good for our environment — and our wallets! Access to clean energy is on the rise in West Virginia, with schools, municipalities, businesses and individuals investing in solar, energy efficiency and more. Come learn how you and your community can access these opportunities! You’re cordially invited to join us for a free webinar on clean energy in West Virginia! WEBINAR Clean Energy in West Virginia: Tools and Tips to Boost Clean Energy in the Mountain State Wednesday, Feb. 26, at 1 p.m. ET Join Appalachian Voices and the National Wildlife Federation for a virtual training on clean energy focused on resources you can use as an individual or organization to advance and fund clean energy in your community. Even with the Mountain State’s clean energy growth, there are still actions policymakers need to take to allow more West Virginians to take full advantage of these opportunities. Policymakers benefit when they can hear directly from constituents. During the webinar, we’ll also discuss how to effectively communicate your clean energy story to decision-makers. Be sure to join us on Feb. 26! Interested but not able to join? Register and we will send you the recording!', '🌿 Virginia Goes Green: Your Trusted Source for Clean Energy Updates & Actionable Info As Virginia transitions into a cleaner, greener state, we’re here to make sure residents stay informed, prepared, and empowered every step of the way. With new legislation, utility changes, and clean energy mandates shaping how we power our homes, it’s more important than ever to understand what’s happening—and how it impacts you directly. Our mission is to bring you the latest updates on Virginia’s clean energy movement, including policy changes, incentive programs, and smart ways to save money while reducing your carbon footprint. From solar power and home energy reports to electrification efforts and local utility insights, we break it all down into clear, actionable information. Whether you’re a homeowner looking to cut your energy bills, a renter curious about your options, or simply someone who wants to do your part for the environment, our content is designed to guide you through the transition with confidence. Learn how to take advantage of free energy assessments, explore rebate programs, and stay ahead of the changes shaping Virginia’s energy future. We believe every resident deserves to benefit from this shift toward clean energy—not just big businesses or early adopters. That’s why we’re making it simple, accessible, and engaging. Get plugged in and power up with the knowledge you need to thrive in Virginia’s green revolution. Follow us for real-time updates, tips, and the tools you need to move forward in a cleaner, brighter Virginia.']

Name: 25_women_wecan_turtle island_indigenous
Representative_Docs: ["Day 2 of the Global Women's Assembly for Climate Justice: Path to COP30 and Beyond, June 24, 2025. The Global Women’s Assembly for Climate Justice brought together over 125 grassroots and frontline women leaders in all their diversity, global advocates, thought leaders, and policymakers representing 50 countries from around the world. During 25 unique panels, speakers showcased a diverse array of visions, projects, policy frameworks, campaigns, and movement strategies with which we can accelerate a bold and transformative path to a healthy and just world. Learn more here: https://www.wecaninternational.org/womens-assembly Panels and speakers for Day 2: GLOBAL VOICES FOR CLIMATE JUSTICE IN A TIME OF UNCERTAINTY — Alice de Moraes Amorim Vogas, Program Director at the COP30 Presidency Team, Brazil —Christiana Figueres, Former Executive Secretary of the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (2010 to 2016), Founding Partner, Global Optimism, Costa Rica —Fleur Newman, Action Empowerment Unit Lead (ACE, Gender and Youth), UNFCCC, Germany —Hindou Oumarou Ibrahim, Coordinator of Indigenous women and peoples association of Chad, Co-chair of International Indigenous Peoples Forum on Climate Change, SDGs Advocate, Chad —Mary Robinson, Former President of Ireland; Member, The Elders; Co-Founder, Project Dandelion, Ireland —Rachel Kyte, UK Special Representative on Climate, Professor of Practice in Climate Policy, University of Oxford, Dean Emerita, Tufts University, United Kingdom —Susana Muhamad, Former Minister of Environment and Sustainable Development of Colombia, Colombia BUILDING A HEALTHY & THRIVING FUTURE: FOOD SOVEREIGNTY —Kahea Pacheco (Kanaka 'Ōiwi), Co-Executive Director, Women's Earth Alliance (WEA), Hawaii, Turtle Island/USA —Mariann Bassey Orovwuje, Deputy Executive Director of ERA/FoEN and Coordinator, Food Sovereignty Program, Friends of the Earth Nigeria and Africa —Monique Verdin (Houma Nation), WECAN Food Sovereignty Program Coordinator in the Gulf South, Turtle Island/USA —Wilma Mendoza (Mosetén), President of the National Confederation of Indigenous Women from Bolivia (CNAMIB), Bolivia —Yasso Kanti Bhattachan (Thakali), Vice Chair of the National Indigenous Women’s Forum (NIWF), and founding member and advisor of the National Indigenous Women's Federation (NIWF), Nepal NARRATIVES MATTER: SOCIAL MEDIA AND STORIES AS A TOOL FOR CLIMATE ACTION AND EDUCATION —Harriet Shugarman, Advisory Council Chair, Our Kids Climate, Turtle Island/USA —Laura Cook, Special Projects Lead, Project Dandelion, United Kingdom —Priscila Tapajowara (Tapajo), president of Midia Indigena Association, Brazil —Vanessa Nakate, Director, Tard Foundation, Uganda REMATRIATING THE LAND: INDIGENOUS SOVEREIGNTY AND THE LAND BACK MOVEMENT\u200b —Bryanna R. Brown (Inuit), Political Advisor and Land and Water Advocate, Indigenous Climate Action, Canada —Janene Yazzie (Navajo/Dine’), Director of Policy & Advocacy, NDN Collective, Diné Bikéyah (Turtle Island) —Pluma Bárbara Moreno Torres (Jíbaro-Taino Nation), General Coordinator, of Organización del Pueblo Indígena Can - Jíbaro de Boriké (CAN) and of Centro de Apoyo Mutuo Jíbaro de Lares (CAMJi-Lares), Puerto Rico —Taily Terena (Terena), Indigenous Rights and Climate Activist, Brazil — Moderation and comments by Dr. Lyla June Johnston (Diné/Navajo & Tsétsêhéstâhese/Cheyenne), Indigenous Musician, Scholar, and Community Organizer, Turtle Island", '🌿🌍 Save the Date and join us for this powerful upcoming WECAN event at the UN Permanent Forum on Indigenous Issues (UNPFII), “Indigenous Women Upholding Indigenous Rights and Leading Climate Solutions”! 🗓️ Date: Wednesday, April 17, 2024 🕒 Time: 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM EDT 📍 Location: 777 United Nations Plaza, New York, NY 10017, USA 📩 Register Today: https://tinyurl.com/WECAN-UNPFII24 Confirmed Speakers, with more to be announced soon: ✨ Dr. Crystal A Cavalier, Ed.D, MPA (Occaneechi Band of the Saponi Nation), Co-Founder, 7 Directions of Service, Turtle Island, USA ✨ Olivia Bisa Tirko (Chapra Nation), President of the Autonomous Territorial Government of the Chapra Nation ✨ Taily Terena (Terena Nation) Indigenous rights activist, Environmental Health Program Consultant for the International Indian Treaty Council, WECAN Coordinator, Brazil ✨ Casey Camp Horinek (Ponca Nation), Ponca Nation Environmental Ambassador and WECAN Board Member, Turtle Island, USA ✨With moderation by Osprey Orielle Lake, Founder and Executive Director of Women’s Earth and Climate Action Network (WECAN), USA Indigenous women and youth leaders from around the globe will converge to discuss and uplift the urgent issues facing their communities and our planet. Speakers will discuss and share the impacts of fossil fuels, deforestation, and the climate crisis in their communities and how they are implementing climate solutions, practicing traditional knowledge systems, upholding Indigenous rights, promoting the right to self-determination, and advancing policies and practices of care and climate justice. Indigenous women leaders fighting for healthy and thriving ecosystems and communities for all generations are a solution to interlocking crises! #IndigenousRights #ClimateSolutions #UNPFII #ClimateJustice #CommunityResilience #IndigenousWomen #IndigenousSovereignty #WomenLead #WomenLeaders #ClimateAction #TraditionalEcologicalKnowledge #Deforestation #EndFossilFuels 🌱🌏', "Opening Day of the Global Women's Assembly for Climate Justice: Path to COP30 and Beyond, June 23, 2025. Representative_Docs: ['What do these two have in common? 💥Dave Min (CA-47) is a first-gen Korean American focused on tackling the climate crisis and more affordable education, while his opponent is a MAGA-Republican who would support Project 2025—eliminating all environmental protections and shutting down the Department of Education entirely. 💥Laura Gillen (NY-04), a dedicated public servant who will vote to restore access to abortion and reproductive care in Congress and wants to ensure that billionaires and corporations start paying their fair share in taxes. Her opponent? A supporter of Trump’s plan for mass deportations and increasing oil drilling across the country. The answer: they are both incredible, people-first congressional candidates MoveOn has endorsed for this election! We’re pulling out all the stops to help our 21 endorsed candidates WIN their seats in the House of Representatives. If we work together, we can secure a Democratic trifecta this November. Not only are we working to elect Kamala Harris as president at the top of the ticket, we are also going to deliver her a Democratically-controlled House AND Senate so that she can hit the ground running to implement her agenda—including restoring abortion rights across the country, making corporations and the wealthy pay their fair share, and defending the middle class. Our innovative election program targets surge voters—young voters and those who need encouragement to turn out again—through a multi-pronged outreach strategy that includes traditional tactics like ☎️phone banking, 📝handwritten postcards, and 📣face-to-face conversations, combined with our data-backed digital ads that are micro-targeted to ensure that only the most effective messages reach each voter. As a grassroots organization, we rely on the support of people like you to go all-in, mobilize the masses, and help these progressive champions win. Will you start a monthly donation to MoveOn now to power our critical work to turnout the vote, flip the House, and elect Kamala Harris? [PAID FOR BY MOVEON.ORG POLITICAL ACTION, http://pol.moveon.org/. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.]', "Comrade Kamala Harris finally has what is very generously being described as a “policy page” on her website. There’s hardly a true thing on it. The dishonesty starts from the top, when she insists her plan is “A New Way Forward.” In reality, Kamala has been in the White House for almost 4 years. We know the results of her policies: chaos, devastation, and destruction. We know the results of her true agenda already – poorer families, the worst border crisis in U.S. history, and the world on the brink of World War III. That’s just the start. Her website claims she’ll: • “Build an Opportunity Economy and Lower Costs for Families” As vice president, she has done just the opposite. She was the tie-breaking vote for the spending that even liberal economists say sent inflation surging. As a result, costs are up over 20% and real wages are down almost 4% since she took office. Under President Trump, real wages increased 8.2%. That’s not all. Housing affordability is at it’s worst levels in American history, and the average middle-class family has lost over $30,000 in real wealth under Kamala. • “Safeguard Our Fundamental Freedoms” – Kamala Harris has supported censoring speech, violating the Second Amendment, and targeting religious Americans. Meanwhile, her administration is weaponizing the justice system against President Trump and Americans around the country who don’t share her political ideology while undermining democracy by opposing commonsense election integrity measures. • “Ensure Safety and Justice For All” – Kamala created the worst border crisis in U.S. history and supports the defund the police movement. Violent crime rates have reach record highs in cities across the country under the Harris-Biden administration, while the migrant crime epidemic has claimed the lives of Laken Riley, Rachel Morin, Jocelyn Nungaray, and many others. That’s part of why the Fraternal Order of Police just endorsed President Trump. • “Keep America Safe, Secure, and Prosperous” – Because of her failed foreign policy, the world is on the brink of World War III. Because of her failed Afghanistan withdrawal, the terror threat against the U.S. homeland is growing. Because of her war on American energy, energy costs have skyrocketed while our adversaries overseas reap a windfall from exporting oil. She has made America less safe, less secure, and less prosperous. Not only does Kamala Harris’s “policy page” fly in the face of the chaos her policies have created as vice president, it flies in the face of her real agenda that she has supported for years. We have the receipts at Kamala2024policies.com This is her policy: • Setting Murderers Free – Kamala Harris actively supports bailing violent criminals out of jail and allowing violent illegal aliens to roam our streets. Case in point: Kamala Harris called on her supporters to “chip in” to the Minnesota Freedom Fund. • More “Bidenomics” With A Communist Twist – Harris was the deciding vote on the inflation-fueling $1.9 trillion “stimulus.” Harris owns this inflation, and everyone knows it. Even liberal economists admit Harris's massive spending spree sent inflation soaring. Now, she want’s Soviet-style price controls that will lead to shortages and breads lines. • More Taxes On The Middle Class – Kamala Harris wants to eliminate the Trump Tax Cuts, which would mean a massive tax hike on middle-income families. Harris was the deciding vote for the “Inflation Reduction Act,” which decreased the “average after-tax income for taxpayers” at every income level. • Abolishing The Border – Kamala “Border Czar” Harris is responsible for the worst border crisis in U.S. history. Now, she wants to abolish ICE, decriminalize illegal border crossings, give benefits to illegal aliens, and let them vote! • Bringing Back The Green New Deal – As senator, Kamala Harris cosponsored the radical $93 trillion Green New Deal with Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, which estimates show could kill up to 2 million jobs and cost an average family $165,000. • “Freedom” For Violent Criminals – Kamala will continue her record from her time as a prosecutor, where San Francisco police officers said she was slow to prosecute numerous murder suspects and said she regularly offered favorable plea deals to resolve cases out of trial. • Defunding Police And Fewer Cops On The Street – Kamala Harris was proud to join the “Defund The Police” movement in 2020. She boldly said the protests “should not” let up and applauded then-L.A. Mayor Eric Garcetti for defunding the LA Police. • A Socialist Takeover Of Healthcare – Harris supports abolishing private insurance. She even cosponsored Bernie Sanders’ $32 trillion socialist healthcare takeover bill. • Giving Social Security And Medicare To Illegal Aliens – Kamala believes illegal aliens should receive taxpayer-funded benefits, and while she’s at it, she wants to expand the government takeover even if it puts over 50% of rural hospitals out of business. Kamala Harris is weak, failed, and dangerously liberal. She does not deserve a promotion. On November 5th, the American people will tell her, “you’re fired!” Comrade Kamala Harris is weak, failed, and dangerously liberal. She does not deserve a promotion. On November 5th, the American people will tell her, “you’re fired!”", '"The New York Times recently published an article entitled, “23 Lesser-Known Facts About Kamala Harris.” It includes facts such as her name translating to “lotus flower,” her height, her love of Doritos, and her enjoyment of cooking. This is the kind of information that America’s newspaper of record believes voters desperately need before making up their minds about our next president. I’d like to offer my own lesser-known facts about Harris. But unlike the New York Times, I don’t actually know the answers. These are such lesser-known facts that only Harris knows them. 1. Does Harris support fracking? When she first ran for president in 2019—some mistakenly refer to her 2020 presidential run, but she actually did so poorly that she dropped out in 2019—she opposed it. In the 2020 vice presidential debate, she stated, “Fracking will not stop under Joe Biden.” Now, she says her opinion remains the same as in the 2020 debate. That would mean fracking will not stop until January 20, when Biden leaves office, because she never said that she personally supports fracking. So, does she? 2. Does she support Medicare for All? When she first ran for president, she supported Medicare for All. Her surrogates say that she no longer does, but she has not publicly opposed it. Her most recent stance, from 2019, is that she supports abolishing private healthcare in exchange for Medicare for All. 3. Which court-packing system does she prefer? In 2019, Harris supported packing the Supreme Court with progressive jurists. Recently, Biden proposed a reform to force senior justices, all conservatives, into retirement while keeping the number at nine. This is a different approach to court-packing than simply increasing the number of justices. Her aides say that she supports this, but she has not stated so herself. What we know is that she supports court-packing. What we don’t know is how she plans to proceed with it. 4. Does she support an unrealized capital gains tax? If you don’t know what “unrealized capital gains tax” means, it’s because it’s a new, brilliant idea by Biden. Most American adults have stock market shares, and Biden wants to tax them even if they don’t sell them. At the end of the year, if Biden gets his way, Americans will have to pay taxes if the value of their stocks goes up, even if they don’t sell them. It’s a genius way if you’re intent on forcing a stock market crash. Harris says she supports Biden’s tax plan. Does she support this? 5. Will she let the Trump tax cuts expire? The 2017 Trump tax cuts are set to expire next year, meaning all income tax rates will revert to what they were in 2016. Harris voted against the tax cuts as a senator. She says she will allow the top rate to go back up, but she hasn’t said whether she will keep the rest. More importantly, what if Republicans insist on making all the 2017 cuts permanent and reject her plan? Will she then let all the tax cuts expire by not compromising, leading to higher rates for middle-class and poor Americans? 6. What’s her plan for the debt? For the first time during peacetime, the U.S. debt is larger than the GDP. This is a moral scandal, and no intelligent person believes that this can be fixed without reforming Medicare and Social Security. Does she think that the debt is an issue, and if so, what is her plan to fix it? 7. Does she still support abolishing ICE (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)? Harris was so outraged by Trump’s approach to illegal immigration that she advocated for abolishing ICE. Let that sink in for a second: A U.S. senator didn’t think we should protect our borders. Then she became the border czar under Biden, although we are told she wasn’t. Has she changed her mind on abolishing ICE? 8. Has she learned the difference between ICE and CBP? Then-Senator Harris advocated for abolishing ICE, despite the fact that CBP (Customs and Border Protection) is the agency in charge of enforcing border laws. Does she know which one is which now?" Read more here⬇️ https://breakingbattlegrounds.substack.com/p/dear-ny-times-23-other-lesser-known']

Name: 28_aviation_sustainable aviation_saf_aviation fuel
Representative_Docs: ['"At the Center for Sustainable Infrastructure, we believe in building a future where sustainability goals can also drive economic growth, innovation, rural opportunity and prosperity. The transition to sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) is a prime example of how we can turn today’s environmental challenges into tomorrow’s economic opportunities. That’s why it’s important to commend @[100044592940488:2048:Congresswoman Suzan DelBene], D-Wash., for her leadership as a founding member of the Congressional Sustainable Aviation Caucus. SAF is a game-changer that represents a shift toward a circular economy where waste products like agricultural residues and renewable energy are transformed into jet fuel, powering both our planes and our economy. And here in Washington, we’re already seeing this future take shape. Nationwide, the SAF transition could result in nearly $800 billion in capital investments and support as many as 400,000 jobs. Rep. DelBene’s commitment to sustainable aviation reflects the kind of forward-thinking leadership we need to tackle the climate crisis while creating economic opportunities that benefit everyone. By advancing SAF, we can unlock new markets, create quality jobs, position Washington as a global leader in the next wave of clean energy innovation, and support the aviation industry, including general aviation, in their ambitious goal of achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2050. Sustainability has to work for the market and for our communities. Rep. DelBene’s efforts in sustainable aviation are leading the way, ensuring that Washington remains at the forefront of building a sustainable, resilient economy for all." Rhys Roth Center for Sustainable Infrastructure Olympia Read more here: https://aviationacrossamerica.org/news/2024/09/15/congress-district-1-delbene-leader-on-sustainable-aviation-fuel/', '"I want to thank U.S. Reps. Angie Craig, D-Minn., and Brad Finstad, R-Minn., for their bipartisan support in joining the newly established Congressional Sustainable Aviation Caucus. Their collaboration and shared vision, regardless of party lines, are a testament to the potential of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and its role in achieving our common goals. First and foremost, SAF is a key to meeting our climate goals, offering as much as an 80% decrease in carbon emissions. But beyond its environmental significance, SAF production can be an enormous boon to our agricultural producers. The Midwest economy could see a significant boost if the federal government’s SAF production targets are met. With the potential to create 224,000 jobs, generate $9.3 billion in income and contribute $71 billion per year to the total economic output, SAF production could be a game-changer for our region. Moreover, SAF would be revolutionary for the aviation sector, offering a practical and feasible solution to meet its climate goals. As a drop-in fuel that requires no modifications to the existing aircraft fleet, SAF is a straightforward and immediate step toward sustainability. The business aviation industry, for instance, which has set an ambitious target to achieve net-zero carbon emissions by 2050, is leading the way in adopting sustainable technologies. It has already reduced emissions by 40% in just four decades and is now investing in SAF and other innovative technologies like hybrid, electric and hydrogen-powered propulsion systems. SAF is truly a win-win for Minnesota and the Midwest. The bipartisan efforts of Reps. Craig and Finstad to support its adoption show that cooperation across party lines is still possible." Niel Ritchie, former president of the League of Rural Voters. Read more here: https://aviationacrossamerica.org/news/2024/09/02/a-move-toward-sustainability/', 'Letters: Sustainable aviation fuel could be next big industry in Louisiana November 11, 2024 U.S. Rep. Troy Carter, D-New Orleans, has taken bold steps to support Louisiana’s agricultural and energy industries by introducing the bipartisan Sustainable Aviation Information Act and helping to establish the Congressional Sustainable Aviation Caucus. These efforts are helping put Louisiana at the forefront of sustainable aviation fuel development — a win for both our farmers and the environment. SAF is an innovative biofuel made from renewable resources like crops and waste materials. It’s gaining traction, and for good reason: By replacing traditional jet fuels, SAF has the potential to cut life cycle greenhouse gas emissions by 80%, helping aviation — including general aviation, a hard-to-decarbonize sector — meet its ambitious commitment of net-zero emissions by 2050. But this move is about more than the environment, it can supercharge rural economies across our state. For our farmers, SAF means new demand for the crops we already grow, like corn and soybeans, as well as agricultural residues that often go unused. These new markets can provide another income stream for our hard-working farm families, strengthening the foundation of our rural communities. It’s a win-win: We reduce atmospheric carbon while creating economic opportunities. And Louisiana is poised to play a big role in this growing industry. The bipartisan Congressional Sustainable Aviation Caucus, including Carter as a founding member, represents the broad recognition across party lines and across sectors of the benefits of SAF from the environment to our farmers to the broader economy. RICHARD FONTENOT President, Louisiana Farm Bureau Federation Baton Rouge, LA Read more here: https://www.nola.com/opinions/letters-sustainable-aviation-fuel-could-be-next-big-industry-in-louisiana/article_2630cf9c-8cd3-11ef-b191-670d372ed7a4.html']

Name: 30_epa_endangerment finding_endangerment_finding
Representative_Docs: ['Earthjustice President Abigail Dillen writes in Rolling Stone about the impact of the Trump administration’s decision to repeal the Endangerment Finding — the most important provision authorizing the federal government to fight climate change: \u200b\u200b “President Donald Trump’s administration has officially put Americans on notice that it will be doing nothing to address climate change. Actually, worse than nothing, the Environmental Protection Agency also announced that it will soon be taking back everything it had done to date to address the biggest source of climate pollution in the country." \u200b \u200bThe EPA is required to protect the American people from dangerous pollution. Trump and EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin are abandoning this mission. \u200b \u200b We will use every legal means available to challenge this move. We’ll see the Trump administration in court.', 'More drama on the way - this time the planned gutting of research at the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency. If you want to deregulate, no better way to do it than getting rid of the data that makes environmental regulations necessary. “According to documents reviewed by the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology and reported by the New York Times, the Trump administration plans to dissolve the EPA’s entire Office of Research and Development (ORD) and cut up to 75% of its workers, placing the rest elsewhere in the agency. “Chemists, biologists, toxicologists, atmospheric scientists, and other experts at the Office of Research and Development are responsible for ensuring that EPA rules that support public health—from PFAS limits in drinking water to air quality standards—are guided by independent research and backed by data. The office runs multiple research programs studying air and water pollution, public health, homeland security, climate change, and chemical safety.” An excerpt from The NY Times coverage: “The E.P.A.’s science office provides the independent research that undergirds virtually all of the agency’s environmental policies, from analyzing the risks of “forever chemicals” in drinking water to determining the best way to reduce fine particle pollution in the atmosphere. It has researched synthetic playground material made from discarded tires; found that hydraulic fracturing, or fracking, can contaminate drinking water; and measured the impact of wildfire smoke on public health. The office also helps state environmental agencies figure out how to address algae blooms, treat drinking water and more.”', "W4-News - Comply with President Biden's Justice 40 mandate - 9/9/2024-ORG W4-News - Comply with President Biden's Justice 40 mandate - 9/9/2024-ORG Press Conference on Monday, September 9, 2024 Location: Department of Energy & Environmental Protection DEEP Headquarters (Outside) - 79 Elm Street - Hartford, CT 06103 Purpose: Reveal Justice 40 Initiative and the results of the CDM Smith Report. To discuss the non-compliance of the Biden Administration’s environmental justice mandate. The Connecticut Justice 40 Alliance, in collaboration with the NAACP Legal Defense Fund (Washington, D.C.), activists and environmental justice advocates are appealing to the City of Hartford, the State of Connecticut and the Environmental Protection Agency to comply with President Biden's Justice 40 mandate, and to release and distribute 40% of federal infrastructure funding, and any other funding necessary to make necessary, critical rehabilitation to Hartford's North Park River, pump stations and combined sewer systems in order to prevent flooding and sewage overflows in the North End of Hartford, CT. With the recent destructive flooding in other parts of Connecticut, Hartford's residents are concerned that MDC's work and repairs to stop the decades of sewage overflows and flooding, will be ignored and possibly defunded. Over the years, some work was done in the North End, but it was inadequate. Furthermore, climate change has led to increasing, torrential rainstorms, which has the potential to cause the same type of ruins experienced in Connecticut on August 18, 2024. There is a pattern of neglect and mistreatment of Black communities, despite the CDM Report which details the solutions. It is time for the City of Hartford, the Metropolitan District Commission (MDC), the Department of Energy and Environmental Protection (DEEP), Department of Public Health (DPH) and the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to take immediate action and work collectively to address these issues. This means complying with Justice 40 laws. Thank you. Representative_Docs: ['Agri-Pulse Newsmakers: May 30, 2025 Senate Republicans will spend the coming weeks trying to agree on changes to President Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill. We asked Clinton and George W. Bush administration ag secretaries, Dan Glickman and Mike Johanns, about how reconciliation elements may change in the Senate and the fate of the traditional rural-urban farm bill coalition. "It looks to me like the coalition broke up," Johanns says. Glickman and Johanns also discuss the Make America Healthy Again report, President Trump\'s tariffs and USDA staff reductions. Want to receive Newsmakers in your inbox every week? Sign up for free! http://eepurl.com/hTgSAD #agriculture #tariffs #reconciliation #USDA #FarmBill #MakeAmericaHealthyAgain', 'Agri-Pulse Newsmakers: July 19, 2024 - Special coverage from the Republican National Convention Delegates, elected officials, farmers, and industry representatives gathered in Milwaukee for the GOP Convention this week. We dove into discussions about trade, immigration, and energy proposed in the GOP platform that could change the policy landscape after the November election. Kellyanne Conway, Donald Trump’s 2016 campaign manager and also his former counselor, joined the show to discuss the role rural voters could play in the 2024 election. She also shared her perspective on action Trump could take as president to balance trade, including potentially providing a bailout for farmers similar to his last presidency if retaliatory tariffs impact ag markets. Then, House Ag Committee Chair Glenn Thompson and Senate Ag Committee Ranking Member John Boozman join the show together to discuss their outlook on what a Trump-Vance administration could mean for agriculture. They look forward to the opportunity for a changing regulatory environment for agriculture and immigration reform. Want to receive Newsmakers in your inbox every week? Sign up for free! http://eepurl.com/hTgSAD', 'Agri-Pulse Newsmakers: 5/23/25 House Republicans passed their reconciliation bill by a one vote margin. The bill boosts farm programs and extends key tax breaks but cuts SNAP program spending. House Ag GOP Committee Chairman Glenn Thompson joins Newsmakers to talk about why the bill is so important for agriculture. He also discusses the impact of federal downsizing at U.S. Department of Agriculture and whether farmers should be worried about the president’s trade policy. Then, Jim Richards with Cornerstone Government Affairs and Anne Simmons with The Peterson Group, Inc. discuss the specifics of reconciliation and whether comprehensive farm bills are a thing of the past. Want to receive Newsmakers in your inbox every week? Sign up for free! http://eepurl.com/hTgSAD #reconciliation #tariffs #agriculture #TrumpAdministration']
