Stan McCauley Owner and Chief of Operations Light Source Productions • AccessTVNetwork.com • AccessTV.org • AccessTV.us • #AccessTV.org • @accesstv_org • #needacameramancallstan8609449797 www.JStan1.net - Phone: 914.ACCESS1 - Let us document your next event: https://youtu.be/ZG-01EhPc-U", 'Today was the day of the tenth annual elite recruitment drive, and every graduating student at Lithium Mine Middle School was gathered in the gymnasium, waiting for the elites to arrive with the medicine that would decide their fate. The elites were the idols of the Golden Dragon nation, super humans with magical abilities that were born from the Divine Injection that the students were going to receive today. None of the children of the miners had ever seen one before, unless you counted the Mayor, who gave speeches a few times a year, and the excitement of having them right here in their school had been all that the students could talk about for months. So, one could imagine the disappointment they felt when it was not some famous idol, but a group of elites from the military that showed up to perform the injections. But although these weren\'t the singers or movie stars that they all knew, it was no secret that these military elites were saviours that protected their nation against hostile armies and the ever present threat of magical beasts. The television in the café and the teachers at the school both told them so. While they had never seen an elite in person, they had seen magical beasts, and quite often. There were earth mice hiding all over the mining town, and the farmers nearby had been raising monstrous boars for generations. But until very recently, it was only the power of technology and the holy magic of the church\'s High Priests and Priestesses that could keep the wild beasts at bay. However, when the more powerful monsters came, you hid, or you died. That was the way of life for the humans of the Golden Dragon nation, and indeed, most of the humans of the world. Karl fidgeted in his seat as he waited for the nurse to come and administer the serum shot. Today was the biggest day of a young student\'s life, the day that they would be administered the Mana Awakening Serum, better known as the Divine Serum, and find out if they would gain the sort of abilities that could change their lives, or if they would be stuck as common workers in a lithium mining town for the rest of their lives. This was also Karl\'s last day at the government run school before he either started working full-time, or with any luck, left this decrepit mining town. If he was compatible with the Serum, he would be off to the Golden Divine Academy tonight, and ready to train his new skills as a defender of the Golden Dragon Nation. They had already sat through an hour of speeches by the Principal, reminding them of their duty to the nation, the glory of the Elites, and the importance of this chance that they were given. After all, they were just the children of common mine workers, dirt floor poor and unlikely to ever be anything else. Karl closed his eyes as the nurse rolled the cart with an open briefcase on it next to him, and then a violent pain shot up his arm and the world briefly went blank. Agony spread to every cell in his body, like he was being torn apart from the inside, and blood clogged his lungs, leaving him drowning while sitting in the metal folding chair of his school\'s auditorium. This was wrong, the shot was only supposed to hurt as the needle poked you, not like this. As his consciousness faded, Karl realized that he was likely to be the one in a hundred, the rare fatal reaction to the shot which sorted out the defenders of the nation from the common people. But after a few seconds, his breathing cleared, the pain began to fade and his eyes fluttered open. A few seconds after that, Karl regained consciousness, with pain still coursing through every cell of his body. The nurse\'s deep crimson eyes, a side effect of her own Serum injection, were staring directly back at him, and a slight smile was on her face. "There you are. I thought that we lost you there for a minute. It\'s a good sign, zero casualties at this stop always means there is a powerful one in the bunch." The woman moved back, and Karl took in the smartly pressed green military uniform, pencil skirt and heels. It was all familiar, but all wrong. Did he lose some of his memories after the injection? Or was there something wrong with his eyes? On second thought, there was definitely something wrong with his eyes, the world was still a little blurry when he didn\'t have someone to focus on. He flinched as his head began to pound again and a new wave of pain wracked his body, but the military woman with the strange red eyes seemed unconcerned as she stepped to a chubby young girl near him and took a large needle full of a glowing golden liquid out of the briefcase on her cart. Without a word of warning, she jabbed it into the girl\'s arm, and the young girl directly fainted, then slowly recovered her composure. A quick glance down at his hands showed Karl a familiar bronze tan, but the scars and peeling skin from a childhood spent working in the mines after class were mostly gone, replaced with a deep red mark that looked like three long claw marks running the length of his forearm. As he silently stared at the marks on his arm, they became more pronounced, and more realistic, as if the flesh had just been torn open, but the skin was smooth and undamaged under his fingers. After a few minutes, the sound of frightened children fell silent, and Karl looked up at the front of the room, where an officer in the formal military uniform was standing behind a podium, waiting patiently for the process to end. The red-eyed woman joined him, along with the six men in doctor\'s coats, before he began to speak. "Thank you all for your cooperation. This year\'s choosing trial has completed, and those of you without the mark may return to your classes as usual. But for the rest of you, congratulations. You have been chosen as the prodigies of the Golden Dragon Nation\'s new generation. The Blessed ones who will lead us to victory over our enemies, with the benefit of supernatural powers bestowed by the Divine Serum." Soldiers poured into the room, twice as many as there were children, and Karl began to panic. His mind still hadn\'t quite grasped the fact that he was one of the fortunate ones yet, the children who were compatible with the artificially induced superpowers, the ones who would become mages, mighty warriors who could split a mountain apart, or even legendary healers that could even raise the dead. At first, the children resisted the idea that they would be grabbed and taken away by soldiers, especially the ones that had failed the choosing. The problem was that they actually needed the help to walk after the injections. The situation was only making Karl more confused, but he didn\'t dare ask what was going on with his body, in case they determined that something had gone wrong with whatever the Serum did to the newly awakened elites and disposed of him. Or worse, sent him back to work in the mines. According to the lessons that they had been taught in class, he should be a mighty hero by now, not a below average sized teen boy wracked with pain and so weak that he couldn\'t get out of his chair. "You look a little disoriented, kid. Just let us carry you, and you\'ll be right as rain after a few days sleep. Just don\'t forget to do the homework before we arrive." One of the soldiers who stepped up beside Karl instructed. That was how he found himself carried into a luxurious train car and placed in a private room complete with its own desk and a call button on the wall that was marked with "Room Service". Unfortunately for his plan to understand why this process was so painful, the moment that his head hit the pillow, Karl was asleep. How long he was out, he had no idea, but when he finally awoke, there was a stack of papers on the desk, along with a small textbook waiting for him to read it. [So, you\'ve awakened your powers] was the title of the book, and the cartoonish cover made it clear that it was aimed at children. Not that he was old, he would only be fourteen this year, but being the last in his class to hit puberty, his body was much younger looking than average. All he had going for him was a handsome face, and even that had gotten him beaten up a time or two. But now that he had the Divine Serum on his side, everything would change. Slowly, he opened the textbook to see what was inside. [So, you\'ve awakened your powers. Congratulations, and welcome to the upper echelon of society, the elite five percent that have been found compatible with the awakening serum that will soon activate the latent magical powers in your bloodline, passed down from the time of our Nation\'s founding by the Immortal Golden Dragon itself. Though you have all learned about it in class, there are a few things that you don\'t yet know. First, your powers won\'t fully awaken until you use the first skill related to your specialty. Just follow the guidelines in this text, and you will discover the primary awakening method for your abilities. Once you have finished that most basic task, you can begin the homework assignment.] Karl read the first page three times before he flipped the page. Next up was a listing of different marks that the awakened classes should have, and most of them seemed self-explanatory. Ice shards, fire, blades, shields, paws, bows, and even an ornate fan were all detailed with page numbers that would lead the students to their awakening methods. But there was nothing that resembled claw marks. So, instead he turned to the homework assignment, hoping for answers. But that was even more useless, it was all about the student\'s abilities. How strong they were, the description, activation times, speed, energy usage, side effects. In short, he couldn\'t fill it in at all. All the marks seemed so obvious as to what sort of abilities they represented. Even the red aura around a pair of axes was clearly a berserker in Karl\'s mind, but the claw marks didn\'t make much sense. There was a similar one with an animal paw that was some sort of druid shaman, but nothing that was as simple, but confusing as his. Was he supposed to be a punching bag for monsters? That didn\'t sound right at all, the book said that these marks represented superpowers. But after a few hours, there still wasn\'t any clear answer as to what he was supposed to do. It was time to call for one of the soldiers and get some answers before the train reached its destination. While Karl was trying to find a way to awaken his powers, the guards were making the rounds, checking which new students were already awake. Those with the most powerful bodies always woke up first, but it had only been half a day of travel, and they weren\'t expecting anything yet. "Sir, there is one active room already. Room 12a, a boy named Karl, no family name recorded." The patrol guard reported back to his commander in the front of the train. "What is his class? Have we seen him activate any skills yet?" The General asked. "Nothing yet. He appears to have a nonstandard Class marking, three claw marks." The guard replied. The General gestured for his assistants to go look for the details on this class, but even after a few minutes had passed, there was still no news. Other markings in their records were close, but nothing exactly like that one was found. "Well, then he will have to awaken on his own. I hope that the boy has good luck or thick skin, he\'s going to need it if he turns in a blank sheet for his assignment when he gets to the Academy." It wasn\'t unheard of for a student to get the mark but fail to awaken their powers immediately, even if they followed the instructions. Some would have gotten a nonstandard starting skill, and some were just worthless at using the power that had been given to them. The General even recalled a case where a child had awakened the Mage class, but didn\'t have enough mana to cast any spells until nearly the end of the first semester. In the end, that student had proven to be a dismal failure, and had run away during a school outing to live the life of a commoner without ever getting past the beginning of the first level of his training. That was the fate of many of the students who never learned to awaken their skills. Either because they were completely unsuited to the abilities that they had received, or because they had a marking that no guidance could be issued for, like the three claw marks boy in room 12a. The Divine Serum was a modern invention, created after extensive research of an astonishing archaeological find at a holy site under an ancient World Dragon Cathedral had detailed the inner workings of an ancient Divine Device known as the System Stones. The device itself was broken, but an initially unidentifiable power within it remained. Using the stones as a guide, and after decades of study, the Serum had been created to attune humans to that power and give the Golden Dragon Nation a chance to overtake the military might of their neighbours and change the fate of their citizens. What did it matter if the Ocean People had water Magic to guard their ships against the rockets and guns of the Golden Dragon fleet? Now, they had mages of their own. Even if the untamed monsters of the Beast Lands attacked, they had warriors and guardians with powerful skills that could cut down the most ferocious magical creatures with ease. That was the strategic value of the Divine Serum, and the reason that every student who was found to be compatible was taken in by the military and intensively trained in the duties and responsibilities that came with being a protector of the nation. Life wasn\'t all military training and duty, though. If it were, there was an absolute certainty that the serum compatible and freshly empowered future guardians of the nation would rebel against authority and either turn traitor or stage a coup. So, they were treated as the legendary resources that they were, able to live in luxury all their lives on the condition that their power was enough to justify their salary. Once they could pass all the tests that the Golden Divine Academy placed before them, they would even receive official titles from the church, and a collection of legal and social benefits to match. Society had always belonged to the rich and powerful, but now the phrase had taken on an entirely different meaning. Karl was blissfully unaware of the topic of the observers\' conversations, and while they were discussing the likelihood that he might not be able to awaken his powers in time to keep up with his classmates, Karl was preparing to ask the passing guards for advice. "Sir? Pardon me, but my skill marking doesn\'t seem to be in the book. Could I be missing a page?" He asked as the uniformed guard passed by again. "Not in the book, eh? Tough luck there, son. The book only covers the common markings, the ones that ninety percent of the new students get, but there are others possible. The injections are a mysterious divine force, and sometimes they give out results that nobody understands. The best I can tell you is to try everything, and whatever feels right probably is. If you\'re lucky, you\'ll awaken some powers before your classes start. Between me and you, you want to awaken those powers before you get to the Academy, the elite students are particular about the power rankings, and if you don\'t, you\'ll be starting school from the very bottom." "Thanks. But have you ever seen a marking like this?" Karl asked curiously. The Guard rolled up his sleeve and showed off the picture of a bear paw, the mark of a Druid, and according to the guide, a nature magic user with an affinity for animals. "Since it\'s a little like mine, and seems to involve animals, perhaps try things related to either animals, or unarmed combat. There isn\'t much you can do to attune with nature on a busy train, but the windows open a little if you need some fresh air. That\'s what I had to do, I couldn\'t awaken my powers without some attachment to nature. In fact, I didn\'t awaken them on the train at all, they woke up as soon as I touched the trees on the way to the Academy Gates." The guard explained. "Thanks for the help." Karl called as the guard walked away. He didn\'t seem to be big on talking, or perhaps he just didn\'t want to get Karl\'s hopes up. But the advice gave him something to go on. Karl flopped on his bed and looked out the window, to see if there was any sort of hint as to what his powers were supposed to be. The guard had a point, and the claw marks definitely had to refer to some sort of animal. Humans didn\'t leave marks like that. So, the first thing that he tried was focusing on adding claws to his hands. "Claw!" "Nope, yelling out skill names isn\'t going to do it. How do the other classes activate their skills?" Karl complained, unaware that the bored supervisors in the main car were watching through hidden cameras and laughing at his antics. [Page 2, Magic Classes Open the box of casting assistants under your bed using the thumb of your left hand, and extract the appropriate casting medium to begin attempting to activate your first spell.] "This doesn\'t look like the sort of marking that a spell caster should have, next page." [Page 3, Combat classes Open the box of practice weapons under your bed with the index finger of your right hand and choose the most appropriate weapon for your desired skills.] That sounded more likely, so Karl knelt on the ground in front of the bed and unlocked the upper drawer, where he found a collection of simple weapons, and even some more obscure ones, like throwing knives, single-handed crossbows, and some sort of axe on a chain that looked like it would be more dangerous to the user than anyone else. He was, unfortunately, not yet among the physically gifted, so Karl picked a short sword from among the weapons while he cursed being the last in his class to enjoy the full benefits of puberty. He had never actually held a sword before, but this one felt right, and Karl gave it a few tentative swings before trying for a more meaningful strike toward the door. [Pet Skills Not Available. You must record a pet.] Karl blinked slowly as he worked to process the idea that had come to him as if implanted in his mind. It was a message directly into his thoughts, obviously from the effects of the marking, and now he somewhat knew what he needed to do. But how did he record a pet? It couldn\'t be as simple as just writing it down, could it? Or maybe he needed to do something to memorize it? But first he would have to find some sort of animal. This was a shiny new military train, not the company houses at the mines, there weren\'t going to be any mice. If he had to touch the animal for the skill to activate, this could be much more difficult than he had been expecting. The obvious choice would be to try writing something down, and to see if that worked. Maybe there would be a book in the spell casting equipment. The drawer of tools slid open, and Karl stared in amusement at the wide variety of strange items held inside. He had absolutely zero clue about how these were supposed to be used. Some of them didn\'t even look like they were anything special, like this silly snow globe with no base. What were they going to do with that? Sit and ponder their Orb? Karl picked the surprisingly heavy glass ball up in his hand, and suddenly, it lit up with a bright white light. [Taming Space viewing is not available. Please Record A Pet.] At first, the message that appeared in his thoughts made no sense, but after a few seconds of focus, Karl could feel the change. In his mind, a vast emptiness was forming, giving him a sense of eternal power beyond anything he had ever imagined. "What about a Dragon? Can I have a Dragon?" Karl thought, trying to will one into existence in the taming space. Nothing. Of course, it wasn\'t going to be that easy. Karl was deep in thought when someone touching his arm brought his attention back to the present. "Did you have any luck?" The patrolling guard asked, looking at the two open drawers, and the sword on the mattress. "Yes and no. I get the feeling that the blade is the right choice, but I\'m lacking something to activate the skill. None of the other weapons I looked at are really appealing, though." Karl sighed. "Well, keep at it, you\'ll find something that works for you soon. If you\'ve already got an idea, you\'re ahead of the others. Most of them are still asleep." "That\'s something, at least. Maybe I can figure this out before we get to the Academy after all. I don\'t suppose there is a kitchen here? I always think better on a full stomach." Karl asked hopefully. The guard smiled and gestured down the hall. "You woke up before the main kitchen for students was ready, but they won\'t complain if I lead you out of the room for a little something. You\'ll learn soon that the food at the Golden Divine Academy is somewhat special, compared to what you are used to. The magical abilities that the elites use put a huge strain on the bodies of their users, and they need the energy of magical plants and beasts to recover quickly. Trust me, you\'re in for a treat this time." They walked down the hall to another train car, past a half dozen rooms identical to the one Karl was in where other students from his class were sleeping soundly, and into an empty dining room, past which a kitchen could be seen. "Just grab what you want and set it by the grill, I\'ll get to you in a minute." The cook called from the freezer, not realizing that there was someone other than staff in the room. Karl didn\'t mind though, it wasn\'t like this was some fancy restaurant, it was more of a common area for the staff to serve themselves, with an actual cook on duty so that those who shouldn\'t be allowed in a kitchen didn\'t starve. Karl collected a pair of small steaks, a bowl of rice, a collection of assorted vegetables, anything that looked and smelled good. Then he noticed a small pile of oversized white eggs, and grabbed one to add it to the plate. [Suitable Pet Target Found: hatching] The egg directly vanished from his hand, leaving Karl staring at the food in confusion and wondering just how long it would take for a monster egg to hatch in a magical void in his mind. Would it even hatch? The thoughts said so, but they weren\'t giving him many details. A small straw nest slowly appeared in his mind, with a single large white egg inside. Over the course of the next few seconds, the Egg trembled, and then cracked open, shocking Karl into taking a step backward, though the sight was in his mind. He bumped into the guard, who thought he might be faint from hunger, and stabilized himself. "Sorry about that." Karl mumbled, while the guard gestured to the cook and led Karl to a table. "Don\'t worry about it. Everyone is a bit off for the first few days after they wake up. Especially some of the magic classes, the power can really scramble their brains. Just focus on solving those strange markings of yours, and you\'ll be fine in no time." The cook looked their way from behind his grill. "Having a hard time with a nonstandard marking? That\'s a rough one. What does it look like?" "Three claw marks on the right forearm. Big ones as well, not one of those wimpy little marks like the nerd classes get." The guard laughed. The cook smiled and turned his hand toward Karl, who didn\'t understand what they meant. They should be talking about a class marking, but with the dozens of visible tattoos, Karl had no idea which one might be the one they were referring to. The cook tapped a small stick tattoo, and Karl realized that it was a mage type Class marking, a wand with sparkles at the end. It was on the top of his hand, and only a few centimetres long. If he hadn\'t seen it in the book, he would have never noticed it at all. "There is a theory that the size of the tattoo has some relationship to the talent of the recipient, but I don\'t believe that\'s right at all. Unless it\'s not related to the mental compatibility, but just the ability itself. Have you got any clues about yours?" The line cook asked. "I felt right with a short sword in my hand, and the marking seems to have something to do with animals, so perhaps I\'m a hunter of some sort, or a park ranger with bad luck." Karl joked. "Well, you\'ll find out once you get your first class skill to work. After that, you can just think the word [Status] and get an impression of how your magical abilities are growing. At least, that\'s how it is for Mages." The cook informed him. 🔥🔥Click to get more chatpers <The First Legendary Beast Master>']

Representative_Docs: ["We are excited to announce the third and last of our Championship Day winners! This impactful project from our Friedberg team focuses on optimizing heat use in their production process. Like all winning projects of this idea contest for Fresenius Kabi employees, it will be implemented within the next 12 months! 💡 The Idea Currently, the production building uses steam generated from natural gas for heating. The new solution involves reusing heat from the production process. By utilizing a heat pump, the system will elevate the temperature of the cooling water's return flow to about 90°C, supplying the heating system more efficiently. To achieve CO2 neutrality, the electricity required for the heat pump will be sourced from green energy. 🙌 The Impact: This innovative approach leverages existing waste heat from the cooling water, making the heating process far more efficient. Since the heat pump will be powered by green electricity, we will make the heating water supply CO2 neutral and save over 200 tons of CO2 annually. The project also promises impressive annual savings of 1,118,000 kWh of gas, which is equivalent to the energy needed to drive an electric car around the world 150 times, and 1,600 m³ of water, roughly the amount needed to fill eight large swimming pools. While there will be an additional need for approximately 280,000 kWh of electricity, the use of green energy ensures a net positive environmental impact. This not only reduces reliance on natural gas but also optimizes resource usage, ensuring a sustainable and environmentally friendly production process. Well done, colleagues! #ChampionshipDay2024 #FutureFresenius #FreseniusKabi #CommittedToLife", "Sonoma Clean Power is leading an effort to build 600 megawatts of new, next-generation geothermal energy in Sonoma and Mendocino counties over the next decade. This is one of the most significant projects in our region's recent history. This Town Hall is an opportunity to learn more and get involved. The goal of this initiative, known as the Geothermal Opportunity Zone or GeoZone, is to secure a long-term supply of affordable, reliable clean energy for our customers while helping to fight the global climate crisis. At the Cloverdale Veterans Memorial Building, on Thursday, February 6th, from 6-7:30 PM, we’ll discuss the potential benefits and impacts of next-generation geothermal energy on our local and state climate goals, grid reliability, energy affordability, local jobs, and more. This event will be in partnership with Supervisor James Gore, Mayor Todd Lands, and others. Food and beverages will be provided Doors open at 5:30 pm Spanish translation will be available Also available via Zoom. Register below: https://us06web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_Fj5MXqovRu6GwF0vZ1nDSA", "A Chilean Visionary's Bold Ventures May Be Vindicated by Digital Economy. Chile, Oct 2024 - Nueva Pudahuel S.A., formerly known as Sociedad Minera Pudahuel, has evolved from being a key player in the mining sector to becoming a pillar of technological and energy innovation in Chile. This transformation, led by Chilean entrepreneur Manuel Cruzat Infante, is positioning the company as a leader in sustainable development, aligning clean energy with the digital economy for the country's future. One of the most significant projects in this new phase is the Kimal Lo Aguirre project by Conexión, which is being developed on 25 hectares of Nueva Pudahuel's land. This large-scale energy project has a nominal capacity of 3,000 MW at ±600 kV and involves the construction of an HVDC bipolar transmission line using LCC technology and a dedicated metallic return (DMR). This infrastructure, approximately 1,500 kilometers long, will connect clean energy sources from northern Chile, particularly solar power, with major consumption centers in the central region, supporting the transition to a more efficient and sustainable energy system. Additionally, Nueva Pudahuel S.A.'s renewal positions it strategically as an international digital hub with the arrival of the Humboldt Cable in Valparaíso. This submarine fiber optic connection will link Chile with Asia and Oceania, establishing the country as a global communications technology node. The cable will enable high-speed, low-latency data transfers, essential for the development of the digital economy, boosting the technological industries and data centers that rely on robust and sustainable infrastructure. Another key element in this transformation is the excellent road connectivity surrounding Nueva Pudahuel S.A.'s land. Its proximity to Route 68—connecting Santiago to Valparaíso—Costanera Norte, and the future road connection between routes 68 and 78, reinforces its strategic position for the transportation of goods and people. These road networks provide efficient access to major cities and ports, facilitating both business logistics and the expansion of new technological and energy activities in the area. Under the vision of Manuel Cruzat Infante, Nueva Pudahuel S.A. not only promotes the integration of renewable energy, but also serves as a catalyst for the growth of the digital economy and technological innovation. The combination of the Kimal Lo Aguirre project's energy infrastructure, the global connectivity of the Humboldt Cable, and its prime road network strengthens Nueva Pudahuel as a key player in Chile's sustainable development. With this comprehensive strategy, Nueva Pudahuel S.A. is establishing itself as a driver of change in the country, leveraging its location and connectivity to foster the digital economy, clean energy, and economic growth, in alignment with global development goals."]

This year, Earth Day is celebrating renewable energy and encouraging the development of energy systems that produce zero emissions, waste, or byproducts – including BESS! East Setauket Energy Storage is looking forward to keeping Long Island clean, green, and healthy for decades to come.', "SunDay is a global day of action on September 21st, 2025, celebrating the unstoppable rise of clean energy. We're showing the world that clean energy works and are encouraging the expansion of solar energy across the commonwealth. Come join us for fun and learning! Workshops 2:15pm - Introduction to Solar PV and Battery Systems - David Gomez, Daily Green Power LLC Including a tour of the Extension Office's 68 KW solar PV system and four Tesla Powerwall batteries. 3:15pm - A Net-Zero, Passive Solar Renovation of an 1880's-era Farmhouse - Andy McDonald, Apogee - Climate & Energy Transitions Displays on site: Solar freezer trailer, solar RV, Electric cars. https://kyconservation.org/sunday", 'Experience an unforgettable Fourth of July celebration like never before! Name: 73_unicef_artisans_children_hunger
Representative_Docs: ['Compassion, Not Control The final step in Psychological First Aid (PFA): Turn Toward Safe Support. In crises—war, disaster, or unrest—helping must be thoughtful, not forceful. We are bridges, not destinations. Over-involvement, even with good intent, can overwhelm survivors or put them at risk. Why this matters: In emergencies, opportunistic harm spikes. History & data show women, children & marginalised groups face increased trauma, manipulation & control disguised as “help.” Predators may pose as helpers or victims, using charm, false identity, or aid to lure. Criminals create psychological traps—flattery, fake support, fabricated authority. Human trafficking & sexual exploitation can rise by 20–30%. Some survivors, too numb or afraid, may resist aid—a response called learned helplessness, where repeated trauma teaches them help won’t last. Some helpers, driven by ego or a savior mindset, may cross emotional or ethical lines—disempowering those they aim to support. Safe, ethical support looks like this: • Offer verified relief options—don’t promise what you can’t deliver • Respect a survivor’s no—trust must be earned, not forced • Share contacts for long-term care—counseling, shelters, helplines • With children, go slow, speak gently, meet them at eye level • Delegate when needed—guidance isn’t abandonment Sometimes, saying “You’re safe now, I’m nearby” is the greatest help. Knowing when to pause, redirect, or say no is as vital as showing up. Also, remember: What is Compassion Burnout? It’s when ongoing exposure to others’ pain leaves a helper drained, detached & overwhelmed—blurring the line between care & self-neglect. How to Avoid It: Balance empathy with boundaries—rest, recharge, seek support. Sustainable care begins with a grounded, well-supported self. #PsychologicalFirstAid #PFA #MentalHealth #India #Bharat #Psychology #Healing #SelfHelp #Compassion #Burnout', "Akshaya Patra refers to both a legendary inexhaustible vessel from Hindu mythology, and the organization's mission is inspired by the legendary vessel, aiming to ensure no child is deprived of education due to hunger. For those who are new the Akshaya Patra Foundation, a large-scale public-private partnership in India that provides nutritious mid-day meals to millions of school children daily. This organization was founded in 2000 to address childhood hunger and improve educational outcomes. The Akshaya Patra Foundation operates large, centralized kitchens, leveraging technology and partnerships to deliver meals to children in government schools and Anganwadi centers across various states in India, aligning with the Sustainable Development Goals of Zero Hunger and Quality Education. They also provide emergency food assistance during crises and support other vulnerable groups like widowed mothers and underprivileged families of young kids. Our las Vegas Chapter us fairly new to this cause and concept. We are trying to help the little children. Please do attend a gala, buy a seat, table or simply donate. Any dollar amount being given to this cause WILL NOT be used for entertainment purposes. 🙏🏽 Donate here: https://apusa.org/las-vegas-chapter/ Please join with friends and family just to support the cause! The link is provided for donations and registrations. If you have further question please leave a whatsapp message on 646-943-2074 #EmpowerFutureGenerations #supportkids #empowerment #fightforfood Be a part of this change. Join us as we work together to create a brighter future for these children. Every dollars counts. Time to meet likeminded people. Network with people who support a cause. https://give.apusa.org/event/akshaya-patra-las-vegas-gala-2025/e688090 #charity #nonprofit #donate #community #fundraising #support #volunteer #giveback #help #donation #fundraiser #donations #education #ngo #givingback #philanthropy #dogood #children #socialgood #charityevent #makeadifference #helpingothers #nonprofitorganization #volunteering #change #charitywork", "Dear supporters, I hope this message finds you well. It is with a heavy heart that I wish you to share the story of families in Port-au-Prince, Haiti, facing extreme insecurity and disastrous circumstances. The families [Pierre, Dumond, Pajotte, Toussaint, Simon, Joseph, florestal, jude, leveille, francois, pierre louis, leozard, apollon] who, due to unforeseen challenges, find themselves in a vulnerable situation. after a huge crisis of insecurity in Haiti precisely in Port au Prince, families who lost their house burned due to heavily armed bandits took refuge in a school as a refuge, their families left abandoned by the Haitian state incapable of managing the situation, here it is famine, health problems, portable water, and especially sleep problems in babies who have just been born, their problems are multiple and their daily life is more difficult every day. As a community, we have the power to make a meaningful difference in their lives and provide them with the support they urgently need. **How \u200b\u200byour donation will help:** 1. **Essential Supplies:** Funds will be used to purchase essential supplies, including food, drinking water, clothing and hygiene products, ensuring the immediate well-being of the family. 2. **Emergency Shelter:** Your contribution will help ensure safe and stable shelter for the family's, providing them with a place to call home. 3. **Medical Assistance:** We aim to facilitate access to medical care and necessary medications for any health problems that family members may be facing. 4. **Education and Empowerment:** With your support, we can explore avenues to empower the family through education and skills development initiatives, providing them with a path to a more sustainable future. **Transparent Accountability:** I understand the importance of transparency in fundraising. Please join us in making a positive difference in the lives of the [Pierre, Dumond, Pajotte, Toussaint, Simon, Joseph, florestal, jude, leveille, francois, pierre louis, leozard, apollon]. Thank you for considering this cause and being a beacon of hope to those in need. With gratitude...."]

Representative_Docs: ["What’s the point of having energy if it’s not available when you need it? Wind and solar are great, but they can't power us alone. Solar panels don't work at night, and wind turbines don't spin without wind. To ensure reliable energy, renewables need to be paired with an on-demand energy source like natural gas. This combination guarantees you have power when you need it most. Do you agree that natural gas is essential for a reliable energy future? Like and share this post if you do! #naturalgas #poweringprogress #energyinnovation #acceleratingrenewableenergy #poweringprogress #energyinnovation #renewables #sustainableenergy #energyshift #energyefficienc #renewableenergy #cleanenergy #gasgas", 'How does natural gas power your daily life? Natural gas is a clean-burning fuel that powers homes, businesses, and industries. From heating water to cooking meals, it’s indispensable in residential settings. Commercially, it’s vital for restaurants, businesses, hospitals and industries like glass manufacturing and auto assembly. Its clean-burning nature also helps in reducing emissions, contributing to a sustainable future. As President and CEO of the Southern Gas Association, I’m passionate about highlighting its diverse applications and importance in our daily lives. How does natural gas impact your life? Share your experiences below! #naturalgas #poweringprogress #energyinnovation #acceleratingrenewableenergy #poweringprogress #energyinnovation #renewables #sustainableenergy #energyshift #energyefficienc #renewableenergy #cleanenergy #gasgas', 'Where renewables fall short, natural gas steps up. Wind and solar power are a part of our energy future, however; they don’t always produce energy when we need it most. That’s where natural gas plays a vital role—stepping in to provide reliable, affordable energy when renewables face challenges. As energy demand grows and electrification increases, a dependable backup is essential. Natural gas offers a scalable, lower-carbon solution to fill the gaps, ensuring grid reliability and energy security and an essential fuel in the evolution to cleaner energy. In the past 20 years, the U.S. has reduced energy-related CO2 emissions by over 20%, thanks in large part to the shift from coal to natural gas. It remains a key player in decarbonization—providing a path to a more sustainable future. A balanced energy mix that includes both renewables and natural gas is the smartest way to meet our energy needs sustainably and affordably. What role do you think natural gas has in our energy future? #naturalgas #poweringprogress #energyinnovation #acceleratingrenewableenergy #renewables #sustainableenergy #energyshift #energyefficienc #renewableenergy #cleanenergy #gasgas']

Name: 76_transit_public transit_commutes_transitsolutions
Representative_Docs: ["Looking for smarter, greener ways to commute on Long Island? Transit Solutions is here to help! We provide customized sustainable transportation programs that make it easier for you to save time, money, and the environment. Whether it's carpooling, biking, or using public transit, we've got solutions to improve your daily commute! https://transitsolutions.org/ #LIRR #TransitSolutions", "On August 20th, voters in Miami-Dade will be asked a simple question: “Does Miami-Dade need to expand its Rapid Mass Transit?” I believe the answer will be a resounding yes. Investing in mass transit is crucial for reducing traffic congestion, reducing pollution, and providing reliable transportation options for all residents so that they may all thrive. We must invest now rather than later to keep up with our growing population and ensure a sustainable, efficient transit system. Let's work together for a brighter, more connected future!", 'Venice has, for years, been seen as a treasure among those centres of civilisation that must not be missed. Its attraction is highlighted by the lack of those thoroughfares that have come to undermine the pull of modern cities. I am talking about roads, of course, and the congestion, pollution and danger that comes with them. This is not good news for cyclists, I know, but I will deal with how I coped with that part of it later. Since it was a city that was effectively built on water, the ‘roads’ are a network of canals, some wide, like the Grand Canal, with room for a variety of bustling traffic. Some, as was the one that led past our hotel, are only narrow enough to allow the passage of a single gondola, or perhaps two, at a squeeze, to move past each other. The gondola is the classic Venetian transport, that stylish high stemmed boat propelled by its no less stylish and skilful gondola. The more prosaic mode of transport is the vaporetto, a diesel powered water bus that we used to get around from one attraction to the other. Even this had a memorable style, coming from the novelty of being able to view landmarks from a floating carriage. The prominent one of these, naturally, was the cathedral of San Marco, standing proudly on one side of its piazza. Caroline’s first thought was to see what it was like inside, but I, thinking of famous artists like Turner and Canaletto, wanted to try my own hand at capturing this famous city. I went across the square to the colonnade opposite the cathedral and, nervously, started to draw. By the time Caroline had come out of the church (delighted with what she had seen) I had just about finished, and I, too was pretty pleased. Proportions are always the key element in freehand sketching, and with this building”s delicate design, getting this right was paramount. While the shape of the church is dominant, I had to suggest the wide space of the piazza in front of it, now beginning to throng with visitors, one of whom was crouching with his camera to get the shot he wanted. Faithful to the moment, I put him in. With the changing climate one of the natural threats to this supposed immortal city, is the rising sea level. This pizza, where I was, regularly floods. The ensuing crisis is not just the harmful effects of depleting tourism, to which the city owes its survival, but to the structural dignity of the city itself. It is a telling irony that, to a place that owed its world dominance and riches to the exploitation of this exciting resource, its future is at risk from it. We enjoyed marvelling at the paintings of Titian, that Venetian master, as well as the interior of the Doge’s palace. We took a trip across the wide stretch of water that separated the island of San Giorgio Maggiore from the rest of the conurbation. From here there is a view across for which I might dare to use that cliche, ‘it takes your breath away’ My painting is a modest attempt to show what I mean. Much of our stay, as you would suppose, was spent ambling around in those areas with smaller canals & pizzettas, where the similarity with other picturesque cities was also marked with hospitable cafes with outside seating. I could sit and sketch to my heart’s content and relish the pleasure of being there. Evening meals were also a pleasure. Finding small, indeed intimate, bistros was our style. I remember the last night, when we found just the right one. When I say ‘last night’ it brings me back to what I said about how I coped with there being no provision for cyclists. I had brought my own two wheeled recumbent bike, which caused some fascination in the airport when I reassembled it when we arrived. Caroline said we should have put out a cap for tips, as it was such an interesting & unusual spectacle! Having left it in storage while we explored the city, my intention was to then retrieve it and cycle home to England. But that is another story. #art #artist #painting #creativewriting #artofinstagram #artjournal #illustrator #sketch #illustration #artoftheday #journal #writer #artwork #sketchart #illustrationart #goodread #cyclist #watercolor #goodreads #watercolourpainting #sketches #sketchbook #penartwork #penart #cycling #writersofig #artistsoninstagram']

Representative_Docs: ['Less than one year will remain on the Climate Clock by the time Donald Trump leaves office. The future of our planet depends on what we do in this moment. We cannot afford to count down the days until the next presidential election — we must act now. That’s why we’re building a movement to resist Trump’s regressive climate policies at the state and local levels. Our climate leaders are delivering the action that we need in communities all across the nation — and we’re going to elect even more leaders to join the fight. Lead Locally is recruiting, training, and running 100+ climate champions who will expand the state and local wall to block Trump’s assault on the environment. We did this the first time. We’ll do it again. If you’re with us, consider donating to fund our fight. Chip in $10 today to fight back against Trump!', "The fight for the planet's future is embedded in this year’s U.S. presidential election in ways big and small. And yet, the phrase climate change is rarely used. Democrats are trumpeting the hundreds of billions of dollars flowing to clean-tech projects; the party's nominee will surely echo President Joe Biden's line that climate change is about jobs. Donald Trump and his supporters on the right have sought to turn clean energy into a stalking horse for Chinese dominance, a driver of rising energy costs, or a globalist ploy. Trump promises to cancel Biden’s vehicle emissions regulations, unlock more oil drilling, and once again take the U.S. out of the Paris Agreement on climate change. A growing cohort of climate advocates view the simmering anti-climate narrative with a concern bordering on panic. They have seen versions of the same fight playing out elsewhere, and it hasn’t gone well. To get through this moment, leaders will need to thread a needle. Read more from Outrider fellow Justin Worland of @[100059280262341:2048:TIME]: https://outrider.org/climate-change/articles/how-avoid-climate-backlash", 'Let\'s talk about our responsibility for the climate. When there\'s a problem, it\'s easy to point a finger. "My sister broke it", "The dog ate my climate." Our fellow Americans are suffering from the impact of enormous storms, and our hearts are heavy for their terror and loss. We are all responsible for the common good. If the people we elect aren\'t helping us prepare to prevent or reduce the risks of climate change, we all end up facing the consequences. We can take individual actions like riding a bus more often, choosing a more efficient furnace, recycling, and composting that make a big impact when enough of us get used to doing them. Above all, we need our leaders to make systemic improvements. Wise policies, smart incentives, and targeted regulations that will protect and steward our natural resources now and into the future. You\'re invited to join me at the Citizens’ Climate Lobby candidate forum in Park City. I\'d love to meet you and talk about how climate awareness affects how we approach our other major challenges like housing, growth, waste management, and water. When: October 8 @ 6:00 pm - 7:30 pm What: Tackling Local Climate Issues – Candidate Forum Where: Park City Library 1255 Park Ave, Park City, Utah #utah #climatechange']

Name: 79_bees_pollinators_pollinator_bee
Representative_Docs: ['June is Pollinator Awareness Month! 🦋 Pollinators are essential to the health of our ecosystems, yet they face increasing threats from habitat loss, pollution, pesticides, land development, and climate change. Their populations are declining, but you can make a difference! By planting pollinator-friendly native habitat, you create a safe haven for pollinators in your own landscape. Pictured below: Pollinators thriving at Moonlight Farm—a National Wildlife Federation certified habitat and a registered pit stop on The Butterfly Highway! Did you know? Pollinators don’t just help plants grow—they sustain wildlife by supporting fruits, seeds, and vegetation that countless species rely on, from birds and mammals to insects and reptiles. Learn more about North Carolina’s pollinators and how you can help: https://ncwf.org/blog/birds-bees-pollinators/', "You're invited to grab a bug net and join the Minnesota Bumble Bee Atlas for a day in the field at Hartley Nature Center! The Minnesota Bumble Bee Atlas is a statewide community science project focused on tracking and conserving native bumble bees. Bumble bees are important pollinators of native wildflowers, like lupine, shooting stars, and milkweed, as well as crops like tomatoes, berries, and squash. Unfortunately, due to threats like climate change, habitat loss, and pesticides, some species of bumble bees are declining. The Minnesota Bumble Bee Atlas provides an opportunity for people in Minnesota to help scientists track the distribution and needs of these essential pollinators. Anyone can participate, and since the project uses catch-and-release methods, no bumble bees are harmed. Volunteers can attend this free in-person training for the Minnesota Bumble Bee Atlas at Hartley Nature Center on Saturday, June 14th, 2025 (1 pm - 3 pm), led by instructors from the Xerces Society. This event will be a great way to learn how to use an insect net, collect data according to project methods, identify local bumble bees, connect with fellow volunteers, and get any lingering questions answered! Accessibility: During the first hour of this event, we will be walking along unpaved trails, which may be muddy. We may step off trail to collect bumble bees, though if you are uncomfortable stepping off trail, you are welcome to stay on trail during the collection period. During the second hour of this event, we will gather around picnic tables to practice bee ID. Boots or close-toed shoes, long pants, sunscreen, and tick prevention are recommended. Before attending this training, it is recommended that you review the online training material or the participant handbook. Materials can be found here: https://www.bumblebeeatlas.org/pages/minnesota Registration required, event limited to 20 people: https://arcg.is/0W1W5H1", 'Want a garden that’s beautiful, low-maintenance, and buzzing with life? Join presenter Susan Carpenter on September 9, 2025, at 6:00 PM at Lake Mills Public Library for an in-person library talk about Native Plants. Native plants are the basis of sustainable, diverse, and beautiful gardens supporting pollinators, beneficial insects, and wildlife. These gardens thrive without fertilizers, pesticides, mowing, or supplemental watering. We will share garden examples, plant sources, and practical tips for garden care. This talk also discusses late season gardening practices (including fall planting) and fall-blooming plants to include. Presenter Susan Carpenter is the Native Plant Garden curator and gardener at the University of Wisconsin–Madison Arboretum. Since 2003, she has worked with students and community volunteers to maintain and monitor a 4-acre garden representing the plant communities of southern Wisconsin. She also leads a conservation project that involves students and the public in documenting and studying native bumble bees, including the endangered rusty-patched bumble bee, Bombus affinis. A graduate of Stanford University, Susan earned her MS in Botany and MS in Science Education at UW–Madison. Her professional interests include plant ecology, ecological restoration, pollinator conservation, and science education.']

Name: 80_concrete_buildwithstrength_buildwithstrength com_https buildwithstrength
Representative_Docs: ['New episode OUT NOW! This week on Concrete Credentials, we welcome Franck Boursier, CEO of Everlasting Homes Group, to discuss the future of resilient and sustainable building practices. LISTEN NOW to learn how innovative concrete solutions are making homes safer and more affordable. https://buildwithstrength.com/resources/building-for-the-future-resilient-homes-with-advanced-concrete-innovation/', 'Sustainable Smart Cities: Built on Modular Foundations As urbanization accelerates, the need for resilient and sustainable urban infrastructure is shaping the rise of smart cities—where digital integration meets environmental responsibility. Modular construction, especially with light steel prefabricated systems, plays a key role in delivering scalable, adaptable, and high-performance infrastructure to meet these demands. Key benefits of modular systems for sustainable smart cities: 🔹 Lower carbon footprint through off-site fabrication and reduced waste 🔹 Compliance with green standards like LEED, BREEAM, and EDGE 🔹 Modular units for smart schools, healthcare, transit hubs, and mixed-use buildings 🔹 BIM integration for data-driven design, lifecycle management, and performance optimization 🔹 Fast deployment with minimal disruption to urban environments 🔹 Flexible layouts tailored to evolving urban needs By leveraging industrialized building methods, modular systems support the creation of smart cities that are efficient, future-ready, and environmentally responsible. #smartcities #modularconstruction #prefabsolutions #sustainableurbanism #lightsteelstructures #offsitebuilding #greenbuilding #urbandevelopment #netzeroconstruction #smartinfrastructure', 'The increase in global surface temperatures will surpass the 2 °C target set by the Paris Agreement unless carbon emissions are lowered to zero by 2050. To date, the building sector is responsible for 38% of all carbon emissions, thus one of the main targets is represented by the development of building strategies that can facilitate the transition toward carbon-neutral buildings.,Construction is a major source of carbon emissions. Moreover, it faces various other sustainability challenges, such as construction waste, construction noise, vehicular traffic near construction sites, dust and other air and water pollutants, and safety and well-being of construction workers. Poorly designed and constructed buildings will continue to affect the well-being of their occupants and overall energy efficiency throughout the building lifecycle.,Due to its similarity to industrialized products, the offsite construction industry is seen as a focus for the transformation of Construction 4.0. Many digital technologies have been applied or have the potential to be applied to realize the integration of design, manufacturing, and assembly.,Soft skills are essential to employability and retention; therefore, if obtained and observed, they can significantly reduce sector-wide turnover. This study aims to investigate and compare soft skills that industry professionals currently possess and soft skills the industry requires and needs to attain.,The labor-intensive nature of the construction industry requires workers to frequently perform physically demanding manual work, thereby exposing them to the risk of musculoskeletal injury (approximately 31.2 cases per 10,000 full-time equivalent workers). Exoskeletons and exosuits (collectively called EXOs here) are designed to protect workers from these injuries by reducing exertion and muscle fatigue during work.,Green buildings (GBs) play an important role in achieving sustainable development goals. However, the implementation of green technologies in building projects has not reached the desired level in developing countries such as Turkey because of barriers stemming from country-related factors as well as factors related to design and construction. The objective of this study was to explore the barriers to undertaking GB projects in Turkey.,Off-site construction (OSC) is an innovative construction method that transfers most of the site-based work to a more controlled environment. Construction waste minimization, speedy schedules, higher sustainability, and better quality are some of the perceived benefits of OSC. Therefore, significant research attention has been given to OSC.,The integration of Design for Manufacture and Assembly (DfMA) into the design process of industrialized construction has the potential to reduce errors and changes occurring after the design has been finalized, ultimately improving overall productivity. Based on DfMA, the designers would need to consider whether their designs meet the architectural and performance requirements, as well as the manufacturing and assembly requirements from assembly and manufacturing technicians.']

Representative_Docs: ['Over the past two days, the Minerals Committee has taken a deep dive into Wyoming’s role in advancing nuclear energy. At the same time, President Trump signed a series of bold executive orders aimed at revitalizing the U.S. nuclear industry and Wyoming is positioned to lead. I serve on the Minerals Committee, and I stand with President Trump on this. Nuclear energy means jobs, national security, reliable power, and real opportunity for Wyoming families. I support the hardworking men and women in this state who want to put food on the table, raise their kids here, and enjoy a good quality of life without being forced to leave in search of opportunity elsewhere. That said, Wyoming’s coal and oil & gas industries built the modern grid and continues to power much of the country. We should be leading with an energy strategy that keeps our base load strong, protects our workers, and embraces the technologies of the future. We don’t have to agree on every detail, but I believe Wyoming should be out front not sitting on the sidelines as America charges forward on energy independence. Let’s lead the way. https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/05/ordering-the-reform-of-the-nuclear-regulatory-commission/', "This is the future of Wyoming? Rising utility rates with every approved industrial wind and solar field project? https://cowboystatedaily.com/2024/08/02/rocky-mountain-power-wants-14-7-rate-hike-for-wind-and-transmission-costs/ My competitors in this race think its perfectly fine with the recent and ongoing approach to approving new wind turbine and solar projects often justified to go hand in hand with water intensive projects like data centers and horse racing tracks. One, Eric Johnston, even justified it as an example of 'American right to free enterprise' ( https://cowboystatedaily.com/2024/07/11/massive-meta-facebook-data-center-splits-cheyenne-state-senate-candidates/ ) . Really? Projects subsidized by The Biden / Harris Administration with our tax dollars and by Wyoming State at the cost to Wyoming rate payers are 'free enterprise'? The fact is, with every new solar and wind project which is integrated into the grids in Wyoming, Wyoming rates go up - even as those projects predominantly serve other states. RMP is showing us that. Blackhills will follow. Every state and country which has added increasing amounts of solar and wind has seen utility rates skyrocket. Wyoming doesn't need these projects - we have one of the cheapest utility rates in the nation (and world) with bountiful energy resources that generate our electricity (coal, natural gas, hydro, etc.) and provide high paying jobs. Intermittent, unreliable solar and wind don't do that and scar our prairie grasslands far beyond those that they destroy with the high voltage lines serving other states. And solar and wind don't contribute to Wyoming's sovereign fund - the predominant source of the state budget and no income taxes. We need a Senator who sees the big picture, understands energy and supports Wyoming energy producers, and that economic progress can occur without destroying Wyoming and leading to higher costs of living. Vote Marc Torriani Senate District 6. marcforwyoming.com", 'This is why Southeastern Wyoming needs new and effective representation in the Senate. The answers from some of the candidates demonstrate being out of their depth in understanding the choices and impacts being made for the future of south eastern Wyoming. We need a Senator who will fight for our water, understands the impacts of projects being proposed and approved, and lets the people decide what Wyoming\'s future and landscapes should look like into the future - not closed door meetings with typical politicians who nod their heads without asking the right questions. https://cowboystatedaily.com/2024/07/11/massive-meta-facebook-data-center-splits-cheyenne-state-senate-candidates/ Since the journalist ( who did a good job in highlighting the issue) didn\'t question me after the event or given an opportunity to follow up, here\'s my communication/email to him this morning- "Leo Good article. I didn\'t see you at or after the event, so I want to take the opportunity for a correction and follow up. I didn\'t state that " the installation of solar farms and wind turbines in other parts of the state have correspondingly raised prices for traditional fossil fuels like coal and oil." but rather that installation of solar and wind turbine farms have resulted in the rising of utility rates" in every place in the world where they are added to grids. As far as the comments by the other candidates there, should I not have an opportunity to counter or follow-up? Both Tara and Darin and woefully out their leagues in understanding water consumption of data centers ( look as close as Iowa if one wants to understand the impacts of data centers on water tables - https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/technology/why-ai-is-so-thirsty-data-centers-use-massive-amounts-of-water/ar-BB1kr6n7 or https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/technology/why-ai-is-so-thirsty-data-centers-use-massive-amounts-of-water/ar-BB1kr6n7 ) and also ask why Colorado concluded it didn\'t have the water resources to host more data centers?. Demand for water and electricity will only get worse with the growth of AI ( https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/technology/ai-supercharges-data-center-energy-use-straining-the-grid-and-slowing-sustainability-efforts/ar-BB1pOPmT ) - who is going to pay for the upgrades to local infrastructure to facilitate both? The average rate payers for water and electricity. Darin should also review the Colorado River Compact and restrictions on the amount of water that Wyoming can utilize - it\'s not unlimited. The other states in the compact (California, Arizona, etc) continue to push for the underutilized part of Wyoming. Again, who is going to pay for the costs to increase the capacity of moving that water from the west of Wyoming to Southeastern Wyoming? As far Kim Withers comments on employment, those 1000 temporary construction jobs will be predominantly from out of state and the \'up to\' 100 full time jobs - again predominantly from out of state - and not local or very high skilled as far as Tech jobs go. The comments from these folks only underscores why Southeastern Wyoming needs better leadership and representation in the Senate. Feel free to reach out to me at any time, Marc"']

Representative_Docs: ['🔑🤔Unlocking Freedom: Housing Solutions is a powerful theme, as housing is such a key aspect of individual and societal freedom. Whether addressing the systemic challenges in providing affordable housing or highlighting innovative solutions to increase access to homes, the goal is often to create environments where people can live with stability, security, and autonomy. Here are a few perspectives or areas where housing solutions can help unlock freedom: See My Blog For Additional Information: https://www.ivhouseintuitive.com/post/unlocking-freedom-housing-solutions 🔑Tiny Homes & Modular Housing Purpose: Tiny homes and modular housing are becoming an innovative solution to the housing crisis, offering more affordable, flexible, and sustainable options for those facing housing insecurity. Solutions: Tiny home villages can provide communities for people experiencing homelessness or those seeking simpler, more affordable living options. Modular housing that is pre-fabricated and can be built quickly, allowing for lower construction costs and faster solutions to housing shortages. Name: 88_birds_bird_warbler_bird species
Representative_Docs: ['There is an ecstatic mechanism in birds that makes them fly upwards in spite of worms. ~ Lawrence Ferlinghetti . Seagulls Laughing, Sea Lions Barking I’m waiting for the full moon to rise. A haze of clouds is streaking through the sky this evening, so I may be wasting my time. The sun is setting behind me as I make final adjustments to my camera. The sea breeze is crisp and clean, and the bay sparkles in the golden light. I fear the clouds will mask the horizon until the moon rises too high for me to catch it glinting across the bay and filtering through the masts and sails. I’m ambivalent, though. The photo doesn’t matter. The climate change news and the pervasive political drama obscures my mind. The facets of the dilemma are always casting shadows. As a teen during the Cold War, the knowledge of nuclear warheads pointed at our collective forehead filled me with intense anxiety. I was jealous of past generations who didn’t have to worry about planetary destruction, but of course, they had their own existential troubles. The moon should appear right now, but I don’t see it. Even if the moon did break through the clouds, I may not be able to photograph it properly, so it’s all just a fool’s errand, or at least it seems so. I can hear the seagulls laughing and the sea lions barking, mixing with the sound of traffic. Dwelling on anxious abstractions doesn’t change a thing. They’re just thoughts in my head while I wait for the moon to appear through a cloudy sky. . . To receive the future: https://robertfeist.com/contact/ . . . #memoir #flashmemoir #mindfullness #stillness #Life’sWhispers #nature #naturecore #MontereyBay', 'Did you know that the birds Aldo Leopold observed decades ago are still teaching us about climate change today? Join us for this special Badger Talk presented by the Barrett Memorial Library, Fontana Public Library, and Lake Geneva Public Library and made possible with the support of Lodge Geneva National! This program is free and open to all and will be in-person only. Although widely recognized for his book, A Sand County Almanac, and for his contributions to modern conservation, Aldo Leopold was also a life-long birder. A well-worn pair of birding binoculars was often around his neck whenever he was in the field. Leopold not only enjoyed bird watching, he also wrote some of his most poignant essays about birds and their conservation. He faithfully recorded many of his bird observations, providing important historical records that allow us to understand how birds are responding to such environmental factors as climate change. Stan Temple will review Leopold’s love of birds and birding and explain why his writings are so important for bird conservation efforts today. About the presenter: Stanley (Stan) Temple is the Beers-Bascom Professor Emeritus in Conservation in the Department of Forest and Wildlife Ecology and former Chairman of the Conservation Biology and Sustainable Development Program in the Gaylord Nelson Institute for Environmental Studies at UW–Madison. For 32 years he held the academic position once occupied by Aldo Leopold. He is currently a Senior Fellow with the Aldo Leopold Foundation. He has received major conservation awards from the Society for Conservation Biology, The Wildlife Society and the Wisconsin Society for Ornithology. Among other recognition of his achievements, he is a Fellow of the American Ornithologists’ Union, Explorers Club, Wildlife Conservation Society, American Association for the Advancement of Science and the Wisconsin Academy of Sciences, Arts and Letters. He has been President of the Society for Conservation Biology and Chairman of the Board of The Nature Conservancy in Wisconsin.', "On November 25, Dr. Steve Roels will be presenting on what could be argued is truly a “State Bird” of Michigan, the Kirtland’s Warbler. The Kirtland’s Warbler is a Great Lakes endemic species that summers and breeds in young jack pine forests, primarily in Michigan’s Northern Lower Peninsula. It winters in scrub forests in the Bahamas. This species was nearly lost to extinction due to fire suppression in its very limited breeding habitat and nest parasitism by Brown-headed Cowbirds. The removal of the Kirtland’s Warbler from the Federal endangered species list as a result of sustained conservation efforts is a victory for the bird and its champions. Yet threats to the species’ future, including climate change and human development, persist. Examining the deep history of Kirtland’s Warbler and its instinctive behaviors offers hope that the bird, despite its fussy reputation, can adapt to a changing planet. Unprecedented research projects are revealing how the warbler’s movement ecology has enabled its survival across millennia and what we must do to support the warbler as it navigates the new millennium. Dr. Steve Roels is a conservation biologist with American Bird Conservancy, currently serving as the Kirtland’s Warbler Program Director and Conservation Team Coordinator. He earned his MA from the University of Kansas in 2011, studying population restoration of a rare prairie plant. He earned his PhD from Michigan State University in 2018, studying bird ecology in tropical forest restorations. He has conducted field research in Michigan, Kansas, Texas, Colorado, Vietnam, and Panama. His scientific work has been published in peer-reviewed journals including Avian Conservation and Ecology; Restoration Ecology; and Agriculture, Ecosystems, and Environment. We hope you'll join us on Monday, November 25, at People's Church, 1758 North 10th Street, Kalamazoo. Come at 7:00 PM for some refreshments and socializing. Snacks and beverages will be provided. The one-hour program will start at 7:30. Masks are recommended but not required in the church. Handicap parking and access is at either the front or the rear entrance. We will have a simultaneous Zoom link to the meeting for those who can’t make it or feel uncomfortable as yet to attend in person. This link will be available shortly before the meeting on our website (https://kalamazooaudubon.org/calendar/). We will also be recording the meeting for later viewing."]

Representative_Docs: ["URGENT❗Tell Your Senators Our Public Lands are NOT for Sale A provision was added to the Senate budget bill that would mandate the sale of MILLIONS of acres of YOUR public lands to private developers. Access Fund is analyzing the land included in the sell-off proposal, and it's clear a large number of climbing areas and beloved recreation spaces are threatened. This bill prioritizes short-term revenue to cover proposed tax cuts and increased government spending over long-term access, conservation and the $1.2 trillion outdoor recreation economy. On top of that, this bill also removes public input on land management, fast-tracks oil drilling and logging, and cuts billions in conservation funding. The good news? Climbers already helped remove a similar provision from the House bill—we can do it again! 🚨 Take 2 minutes to contact your Senator to tell them that our public lands are NOT for sale: https://www.accessfund.org/action-alerts/action-alert-stop-the-senates-extreme-public-land-sell-off-bill This is going to take all of us. Climbers, hikers, bikers, fishers, hunters, skiers, and outdoor recreationalist—MAKE YOUR VOICE HEARD! We climb. We protect. We stand together. 📸: Eric Bowes", 'Tell them we want our f*cking rights. 640 million acres of public land belong to us. Not oil execs. Not hedge funds. Not billionaires. Right now, 3 million acres are on the chopping block. These lands are where we ride singletrack, cast fly rods, climb granite walls, and breathe clean air. They’re ours. And they’re under attack. Congressional Republicans are moving now to gut clean energy and sell off our shared backyard. Once it’s gone, it’s gone. This is a land grab disguised as a budget cut. A theft of our future, our culture, our access. Get loud. Get in the way. Get your reps on the record. 📬 60 seconds. That’s all it takes to defend what’s ours. 🔗 Link in bio to contact your representatives. #protectourwinters', 'Some things are just inherently American: Bald eagles. PB&J. Free speech. Public land. Roughly 30% of the U.S. (about 640 million acres) is public land. That means it’s owned by us, the American people—not corporations or billionaires. These lands include national parks, forests, wildlife refuges, and BLM lands. They provide clean water, store carbon, sustain wildlife, and give us space to explore. Public land is where we cast fly rods, bomb singletrack, carve pow turns, and unplug in the wild. It’s our escape. Our heritage. Our shared backyard. But once it’s sold off or privatized, there’s no getting it back. Right now, powerful interests are working to chip away at these lands. The only way to stop them? Get loud. Get organized. Get involved. 📬 Contact your representatives and demand bold action to keep public lands in public hands. Public land belongs to all of us. Let’s keep it that way.
