Name: 90_georgia_psc_georgia public_service commission
Representation: ['georgia', 'psc', 'georgia public', 'service commission', 'largest animal', 'solar north', 'carolina residents', 'georgia power', 'energy funds', 'commission psc']
Representative_Docs: ['Pollution from fossil fuels damages children’s lungs and causes asthma attacks. But Georgia’s Public Service Commission (PSC) voted to keep burning dirty, expensive, harmful fossil fuels instead of moving to clean energy. It’s time to protect our kids and speak up for clean, safe energy. This fall, vote for a PSC Commissioner who will protect our families.', 'Fossil fuel pollution is causing climate change and extreme weather. But Georgia’s Public Service Commission (PSC) wants to keep burning dirty fossil fuels instead of moving to cleaner, safer, more affordable energy sources. It’s time to vote for a PSC candidate that will protect Georgia’s families. Early voting is open now - make your voting plan today!', 'Thanks to the Inflation Reduction Act, Georgia is leading the country in renewable energy production, helping farmers streamline agricultural practices, and lowering healthcare costs for low income residents. Georgia now ranks second nationally on new green tech projects, with over 20 new projects and over 30,000 jobs created since the IRA’s passing. The bill has spurred $23.88 million for Georgia’s clean energy economy, with the greatest investments coming to solidly red parts of the state, like the 11th Congressional District. In rural communities, the Inflation Reduction Act has helped farmers to adopt sustainable, efficient methods and is providing affordable electricity through local electric cooperatives. Learn more at https://www.newdealforum.org/federal-funds-local-results']

Name: 91_indiana_hoosier_hoosiers_indiana energy
Representation: ['indiana', 'hoosier', 'hoosiers', 'indiana energy', 'homegrown hoosier', '2048 indiana', 'hoosier state', 'indiana conservation', 'energy mix', 'homegrown']
Representative_Docs: ['We harness solar energy to strengthen local economies and build an energy future that provides affordable, reliable energy for all Hoosiers. Our collaboration with landowners, residents, and local businesses across the Hoosier State has contributed to more than $35.3 million paid to local governments, $90.6 million paid to local landowners, and more than $226.2 million in total economic impact for the surrounding communities where our projects reside. Learn more about our impact in Indiana at: https://www.edpr.com/north-america/our-impact', 'PROJECT SPOTLIGHT: Located about 80 miles northeast of Indianapolis in Randolph County, our Riverstart Solar Park is a hub for homegrown energy and a testament to the dual-use benefits of agrivoltaics. 🐑 In addition to hosting some “woolly” vegetation managers, Riverstart represents: ⚡ 200 MW of installed capacity added to the local grid — enough to power more than 38,400 Indiana homes 💧 254 million gallons of water saved each year 🔨 530 construction jobs and 8 permanent positions 🏫 $700,000+ in payments to local governments from 2020-2022 💵 $173 + million in local spending within 50 miles of the solar park Learn more about the project on our website: edpr.com/north-america/riverstart-solar-park', "A proposed solar development, that has gone through years of planning, will add much needed energy and an estimated $20 million in tax revenue to Jefferson County if local officials and residents show their support. Will you join the Hoosier Environmental Council and show up for solar? RSVP and learn more at: https://secure.everyaction.com/NXrKWqFE8k6nv_yy3I-YgA2 Here is why it is important: -Indiana needs more energy to help grow our economy and keep our utility bills from skyrocketing. Solar is the cheapest form of new energy and when paired with battery storage provides needed resiliency to our electric grid. -Coal is the worst and one of the most expensive forms of energy. It pollutes our waterways, the air we breathe and pushes heat trapping CO2 into our atmosphere. The coal lobby is fueling opposition to solar in an effort to make Indiana reliant on coal. For our environment and our pocketbooks – we need to say “YES” to solar and “NO” to coal. -Opportunities like this don't come often. This proposed project has made it through MISO's interconnection que (or received necessary approval to connect to the grid) which takes years for approval. Interconnection approval is difficult to get – we don't want to waste this opportunity for affordable solar. Please register here so we know you're coming and so we can share additional information with you. Sam Carpenter, Hoosier Environmental Council's executive director will meet you there! https://secure.everyaction.com/NXrKWqFE8k6nv_yy3I-YgA2"]

Name: 92_oklahoma_boles_prosperity tracker_tracker
Representation: ['oklahoma', 'boles', 'prosperity tracker', 'tracker', 'oklahomans', 'brad boles', 'oklahoma energy', 'oklahoma economy', 'oil natural', 'alliance legislative']
Representative_Docs: ["74 Oklahoma legislators signed our letter to Oklahoma congressional members backing natural gas, supporting taxpayers and denouncing tax credits for weather-dependent energy sources. We'd like to send a special THANK YOU to Senate Energy Committee Chair Grant Green and House Energy and Natural Resources Oversight Committee Chair Brad Boles. These two continue to stand with Oklahoma's oil and gas industry — thank you for leading by example and joining The Petroleum Alliance in calling for an end to federal subsidies for renewable energy. 🤝", '🇺🇸Energy Independence🇺🇸 I proudly support oil and gas production on American soil—one of the lifelines of our economy and a proud symbol of American ingenuity and strength. The livelihoods of hardworking Americans depend on this vital industry! I strongly oppose reliance on foreign energy sources and stand firmly against unnecessary wind turbine projects that harm our land and fail to deliver lasting benefits. Oklahoma’s economy thrives on oil and gas, providing the critical funding for our schools, infrastructure, and essential public services. Turning our backs on this industry would devastate our communities and our way of life. Elect me, Bryan Logan, for Oklahoma State Senate, District 8. Together, we’ll fight for our energy, our jobs, and our future. 🗳️Primary: March 4, 2025 🗳️Run-Off: April 1, 2025 🗳️General: May 13, 2025 www.BryanLoganOK.com #DrillBabyDrill #OilAndGas #EnergyIndependence #BryanLoganForStateSenate', '💪 President Trump and DOGE are CUTTING red tape and bureaucracy to strengthen the American economy and get government out of the way, 🚨 But here at home in Oklahoma, fake conservatives are trying to create MORE red tape and bureaucracy, which would make our state economy weaker and less competitive. Here’s the latest example ⬇️ Fake conservatives in Oklahoma are pushing radical policies that tell Oklahoma landowners what kind of energy they can and can’t build on their own land – whether it’s solar, oil, gas, or wind. And how much. 🚩 We don’t want Soviet-style “land stewards” and more in Oklahoma. Because Americans don’t stand for that! And it hurts our state’s ability to attract good businesses and good jobs. It’s your land. It’s your choice. And Oklahoma politicians should stay the hell off of it. Especially when it comes to energy. We have to defend our private property rights when our elected officials won’t.']

Name: 94_sunzia_sunzia transmission_new mexico_biloxi
Representative_Docs: ['Have you ever noticed the Zia sun symbol in our campaign logo? This iconic emblem probably looks familiar to fellow New Mexicans – you can find it on our state flag, on license plates, in the state Capitol building, and more. But there’s a deeper meaning. It’s a sacred symbol to the Indigenous people of Zia Pueblo, representing the Circle of Life: four winds, four seasons, four directions, and four sacred obligations. This meaning is at the heart of this campaign, and why I chose to include it in our logo. Because today, our state’s strong desert sun is just as important to our future as it was to our Indigenous ancestors. It powers our robust agricultural economy and serves as a source of power for thousands of our homes. But I know it can power our future, too. As governor, I’ll keep pushing for smart, forward-thinking investments in solar energy. Not just to combat climate change, but to create good-paying union jobs and power homes across New Mexico. With respect for our past and a clear vision for the future, we’ll build something brighter for everyone. No state has ever elected a Native American woman as governor. With your help, we can change that. Even $5 makes a difference and helps us get one step closer to that brighter future. – Deb ☀️', 'BREAKING NEWS: The @[100064469268777:2048:City of Biloxi] has heroically identified the root cause of homelessness: trees. Forget affordable housing, social services, or mental health care—those things are overrated. The real enemy? Shade. Apparently, every time a live oak dares to provide a square foot of cool relief, a homeless person appears like a woodland Pokémon. So now it’s chainsaws at dawn. If it’s green, it’s obscene. If it dims the light, it’s not alright. If it grows a leaf, it’s causing grief. This bold new plan, code-named "Operation: Embrace the Sun", will transform Biloxi into a utopia of bare dirt, exposed concrete, and third-degree sunburns. Officials say the goal is simple: if there’s nowhere comfortable to exist, no one will want to exist here—like the homeless. Mission accomplished. City workers are already training falcons to scream at people who pause too long near a shrub, and every squirrel is now a suspected accomplice. One was caught distributing acorns without a permit. To compensate for the mass deforestation, the City promises to replace every majestic oak with a plastic palm tree from @[100064703979336:2048:Dollar General] and a heartfelt “thoughts and prayers.” Somehow, these plastic trees will still qualify Biloxi for the @[100064777746215:2048:Arbor Day Foundation]’s “Tree City USA” program, because nothing says tree advocacy like a zip-tied piece of molded polyethylene in a pothole. Landscape architects have already begun sketching out plans for a climate-resilient cactus garden in front of City Hall, perfect for welcoming tourists who enjoy the aesthetic of a post-apocalyptic @[100064898313695:2048:Taco Bell] parking lot from the movie Demolition Man. In the near future, the only shade left in Biloxi will come from the passive-aggressive sighs of overheated pedestrians and the spiritual darkness of whoever approved this plan. But look on the bright side—literally, because there’s no canopy left. Whether you’re homeless, housed, or just hopeless, Biloxi’s got one message for you: No Trees, No Shelter, No Problem.', 'Landowners, environmental consultants, a local saguaro farm, and Pattern Energy are collaborating to preserve Arizona’s iconic desert landscape. Pattern Energy’s SunZia Wind and Transmission project will create the largest clean energy infrastructure project in United States history. Construction of SunZia started in 2023 in New Mexico and Arizona. Minimizing the environmental impact on a project of this scale has involved extensive efforts. One example is the work being done to preserve local saguaro cactuses that will otherwise impacted by construction. In coordination with environmental experts, Pattern is relocating thousands of saguaros rather than simply cutting them down. The large, tree-like saguaro cactus, found exclusively in the Sonoran Desert, is closely tied with Arizona’s identity and is an important component of the desert’s ecosystem. Although it’s not on the threatened or endangered species list, “Arizona has strict regulations about the harvesting, collection, or destruction of this species.” That’s why this collaboration is important. “We’ve relocated over 15,000 saguaros and agave throughout the project’s footprint. I think what’s really special about SunZia is the sheer magnitude of the effort going into a project like this,” explains Justin Montefalcon, a biologist at Canacre, an environmental consulting firm working on the project. Pattern and Canacre have also been working with landowners who are involved in the project. “This has been a real blessing,” says local landowner Russ Buhrow. “Our association with Pattern has been financially beneficial to small businesses and the community. I’ve had a good experience working with SunZia. They’ve been very honorable.” Pattern is grateful to the landowners and other partners who have been instrumental in making the saguaro relocation project successful. Together we are demonstrating how environmental stewardship and renewable energy go hand-in-hand.']

Name: 97_hotel_pflugerville_budget_westchester
Representative_Docs: ["FICTION: The Village is running a deficit budget due to wasteful spending. FACT: The Village's Fiscal Year (FY) 2025 budget represents a deliberate and clear investment in essential, long-overdue priorities—NOT uncontrolled spending, but a responsible use of surplus funds aimed at stabilizing our future and ensuring long-term financial stability. - Strong Financial Management: The Village concluded FY 2022 and FY 2023 with a combined surplus of $3.4 million, as verified by independent auditors on February 4, 2025. - Purposeful Investments: In April 2024, the Village approved the strategic allocation of $795,000 from its reserves to address unfunded pension obligations, appropriately staff departments, and enhance operational efficiency. This decision represents approximately 3.3% of the total budget for fiscal year 2025, which is around $24 million. - Long-Term Fiscal Responsibility: While this year's budget reflects a short-term planned deficit, it is a calculated move to strengthen Westchester's future. The FY 2025 budget represents an intentional transitional year for the Village, emphasizing fiscal transparency and accurate cost accounting, setting the foundation for a stronger, more sustainable future. Conservative forecasting and the strategic use of reserves are expected to balance the budget by the end of FY 2025. - Maintaining Healthy Reserves: The Village's FY 2025 budget includes a planned allocation that projects a General Fund balance of approximately $8.3 million, which represents 34.8% of the budgeted expenditures. This level of reserves aligns with Moody's recommendations for maintaining a strong credit rating and signifies fiscal stability, putting Westchester in a favorable position with credit rating agencies. - Proven Financial Stability: The Village has preserved its favorable A2 bond rating from Moody’s. This rating is attributed to recent policy enhancements, timely financial reporting, proactive capital planning, and the commitment of the new management team to address pension liabilities—areas that were previously identified as credit weaknesses. - Transparent Communication & Leadership The Village clearly communicated these budgetary plans and financial strategies in significant detail. Claims that this budget was not discussed or justified are inaccurate and misleading. Trustees had a full four weeks to ask questions, between the introduction and the passage of the budget. Westchester residents deserve leaders who are informed, transparent, and honest about our financial health and plans - not political games designed to create unnecessary fear in a disappointing attempt to garner votes. What is truly wasteful? Overlooking unfunded liabilities, neglecting infrastructure needs and mandates, failing to adequately staff essential positions, and postponing financial problems. This past approach is ineffective—we are now making responsible, forward-thinking decisions to ensure Westchester's long-term success. - References: https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/.../COW... https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/.../COW... https://granicus_production_attachments.s3.amazonaws.com/... https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/.../COW... https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/.../VB_9A... https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/.../VB... https://myemail.constantcontact.com/Westchester-Weekly-E... https://myemail.constantcontact.com/Westchester-Weekly-E... https://myemail.constantcontact.com/Westchester-Weekly-E... https://www.westchester-il.org/.../approved-final-fy-2025... https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/.../COW... https://www.westchester-il.org/.../westchester-afr-23-final https://www.westchester-il.org/.../fy2022/westchester-afr-22", 'Facts about the DOWNTOWN HOUSING & ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITY OVERLAY This is the first of a series of essays I am writing about what is happening in our town. I am starting with the overlay, because I think that is the most pressing issue. It is a long essay, but I wanted to be thorough in bringing up most of the issues. I ran the Riverfront Art Gallery in downtown Petaluma for 15 years. I spent hundreds of evenings over many years, talking to out-of-town visitors on Friday and Saturday nights, before or after they dined in one of our fine restaurants. People came from all over the North Bay, Oakland and San Francisco to spend an evening in our town. I eventually started to realize that we have the most vibrant downtown between San Francisco and Portland. There are indeed problems that need to be addressed in our historic downtown, such as: high rents, long waits for permits (and high permit fees), lack of parking, lack of small retail spaces to rent, etc. Ruining our historic downtown with this out-of-scale, out-of-place, and bland hotel is not worth even the rosiest economic projections that the city can pull out of their hat. Summary of the Overlay Proposal This is a proposal to re-zone specific areas of our historic downtown to make the following changes (among other changes): - change the maximum allowable height of buildings in certain parts of our Historic Commercial District from 4 stories or 45 feet to 6 stories or 75 feet - increase lot coverage from 80% to 100% - allow ground floor residential uses Why is the city doing this (according to them)? The city says that this overlay is something that the city has been interested in to add housing to our downtown, and to economically stimulate our “blighted” commercial district (it’s right there in the name). Lucky for us, the EKN Development Group of Newport Beach has agreed to fund the planning to create this overlay, and since the EKN people are paying the M Group people to do all the planning work, none of this is costing city taxpayers a dime. According to the city, the hotel and overlay will result in more economic activity (making the chain link fences disappear), and increase housing in our downtown. What you need to know. The proposed hotel violates the design guidelines of our historic downtown that were established in 1999. Instead of saying “sorry, this will ruin the character of our town, bye”, the M Group came up with the idea of creating an overlay that will allow this non-conforming hotel to be built. They first tried to make the overlay apply to the entire downtown (including Kentucky Street). But when they saw massive opposition to that, they broke the overlay up to cover only certain parts of downtown and named those zones A, B, and C. By the way, they are not going to approve just one zone. They state that clearly in section 6.7.3 - “Overlay in Area A Only” in the DEIR (Zone A is where the hotel would be). If they only rezoned the one area for the hotel, the city may be vulnerable to a spot zoning lawsuit hence the ruse of choosing three areas and renaming it the “DOWNTOWN blah, blah, overlay”. Before EKN proposed a 6-story hotel with 100% lot coverage (so many coincidences with the proposed overlay), there were no plans for housing in our downtown. California cities are required to create housing plans in 8-year cycles to meet their RHNA (Regional Housing Needs Assessment) numbers. Our 2023-2031 Housing Element plan was adopted by the city on March 20, 2023. The amount of housing proposed for the downtown core in that plan is zero units, so it sounds like housing is not one of the reasons this overlay is being proposed. Call me cynical if you will. Let’s look at all that economic opportunity. The proponents of this hotel say that it will generate up to $700,000 per year in sales tax, property tax, and occupancy tax revenues annually. This number is suspect. When our hotels aren’t full, an overnight stay at the EKN hotel likely results in one less overnight stay at the Hotel Petaluma or the Metro Hotel, I doubt that this is accounted for. In any case, the city’s annual budget is around $288 million dollars. A revenue increase of $700,000 represents ¼ of 1 per cent of our budget. The added funds received from Measure U amounts to around $16 million dollars per year, which is over 20 times more than the highest estimated revenues that would come from this hotel. But wait, there’s more… If our downtown core is zoned for housing units with these overlays, and the location of those units are within a ½ mile of a major transit station (where most of the zones are located), developers can build even taller buildings with “density bonuses”. In that case, the 75 foot height limit could become a 108 foot height limit (or 9-stories). They would also not be required to include a single parking space. They may even build housing in these overlay zones under certain conditions “by right” (even if the city objects). By the way, a planning commissioner asked the M Group representative in a planning meeting last year if they accounted for state-mandated density bonuses in their general plan (they didn’t). Mike Healy wrote a well-reasoned op-ed about this possibility in the Argus-Courier this week (it would have been nice if he had brought up these issues during official meetings, but it is nice to see that he has joined me in opposing this overlay), you can find his editorial here https://www.petaluma360.com/article/opinion/downtown-petaluma-overlay-zone-hotel-problem/ This hotel will provide parking for up to 58 vehicles (all valet parking), yet the parking needs for this hotel exceeds 200 vehicles. There are 93 guest rooms, a restaurant that seats 150, and an exterior event space for 60. This hotel would also remove 3 current parking spaces on the street. Areas located within the overlay zones will become part of a parking assessment district and Section 11.035 of the IZO (Implementing Zoning Ordinance) specifies that sites and structures within the Parking Assessment District are exempt from the requirement to provide off- street parking facilities. Yet if you go to the city’s website and look at “How will parking be addressed?” It says, “The parking and loading standards contained in IZO Chapter 11 would not be modified by the Overlay and would continue to apply to all uses and buildings as under existing conditions.” It looks like a contradiction to me. The Draft EIR assumes that the overlay has been approved, that is why the DEIR says there are no problems with this hotel. It is only compliant if we approve the overlay. It also states that several other locations were suggested for the hotel, but… “There are no significant and unavoidable impacts associated with the proposed project. Accordingly, none of the sites suggested as alternatives would avoid or substantially lessen a significant and unavoidable impact.” Here are the next steps regarding the DEIR for the overlay. You can learn more about the Draft EIR and its findings at two upcoming meetings, more info here: https://cityofpetaluma.org/community-update-for-august-23-2024/ Tues., Sept. 24, 6pm (in-person only) Joint Planning Commission + Historic & Cultural Preservation Committee Meeting City Hall (11 English Street) Mon., Oct. 7, 6:30pm (in-person to comment, virtually for viewing only) City Council Meeting City Hall (11 English Street) or via Zoom Will the city really listen to your input? You’ve got to be kidding right? They are having the meetings so that they can check off the box that says “Community Input”. They are not interested in your feedback. They don’t even have “Public Hearing Notice” signs posted in the Overlay Zones A, B, or C, for what would be the biggest change to our downtown in our lifetime. If one paid close attention to the city’s Dog and Pony Show about the overlay at the Woman’s Club in July of last year, Dave Alden of Know Before You Grow, and Brian Oh from the city, were talking about WHEN this overlay was going to be approved, not IF. Dave Alden also repeatedly asked to crowd to not be so angry, and only ask a question and not make a statement (or words to that effect). So why would you make comments then? The City Council must hold a public hearing on the Draft EIR (which could be continued if they need more time). You should make written comments that state what the problems are with this DEIR so that your comments can be included in the official record. Only information that is in the official record can be considered in a lawsuit, several of us learned that the hard way. WHEN the current city council approves the DEIR on October 7th (remember, they are not listening to you). They/staff/consultants will respond (or not) in writing to all comments received from the public, agencies, Planning Commission, Historic & Cultural Preservation Committee, and City Council members. The written responses to comments become the Final EIR. They will need to then hold a hearing on that collected document (date to be determined) along with any proposed mitigation measures or other Conditions of Approval, or Statement of Overriding Considerations. After voting to approve ("certify") that FEIR Resolution the citizens have 30 days to file a lawsuit in Sonoma County Superior Court. If a judge rules that the FEIR is deficient, it has to be re-written and brought back before the city council again (hopefully with different members by then). The council then has an option to reject any new FEIR. In Conclusion If I am elected as the Council Member from District 6 (where this overlay scheme is located), I will do everything in my power to protect our Historic Commercial District (registered in 1995), and preserve our historic design guidelines. I have no objection to a hotel at this location, but it must be compatible with our historic district. Your vote matters! Lance ----------------------------------------------------- An Endorsement from a District 6 Voter “Lance shows the values that make Petaluma unique among other other cities In Sonoma. He values the environment, the humanity of the city, the beauty and the heritage.” ----------------------------------------------------- To help spread the message of this campaign, consider getting a yard sign and/or making a donation. I am running against two incumbents simultaneously, and changing how our city is run isn’t easy. Get a sign here: https://share.hsforms.com/1i7qhmH6_StOwgo6-NsNsrwrtxgh Make a donation here: https://share.hsforms.com/1Qnm1ZuPYRLOsY5sDTFgTOQrtxgh', 'Two Paths: Oakham Deserves Financial and Operational Strengthening, Not the Same Old Problems Dating Back to 2009 A recent Financial Management Review (FMR) conducted in 2024 by the Massachusetts Division of Financial Services for Oakham revealed something concerning—many of the same financial and governance issues that were identified in the 2009 FMR still haven’t been addressed. Based upon a comparative review of both reports- What Hasn’t Changed Since 2009? 🔹 Lack of clear roles and responsibilities for town officials in outdated or missing bylaws or policies, creating inefficiencies. 🔹 No formal budget process, leading to inconsistent financial planning. 🔹 Inconsistent communication and coordination between town departments. 📌 What’s New in the 2024 FMR? 🚨 Incomplete accounting software transition was delaying financial reconciliations- addressed late 2024 with a brand new software after a failed previous very expensive software purchase created chaos and inefficiencies, as It didn’t communicate with other departments. 🚨 Heavy reliance for several years now on free cash to cover operational costs—an unsustainable practice with no easy solution in place. 🚨 Lack of economic planning, limiting new revenue sources. 🚨 Dependence on contract agencies and temporary fixes to manage our Town Accountant, Assessor and Treasurer/Collector responsibilities. 🚨 A Finance Committee that is fully committed and doing its best, but hampered by member vacancies, which can make more difficult its ability to adequately advocate for Oakham taxpayers and adds more burden on these dedicated volunteers. 💡 Oakham Needs Strategic Leadership & Smarter Financial Planning For over a decade, Oakham has faced many of the same problems unresolved. It’s time for experienced leadership that takes action, engages all stakeholders, working together to improve efficiency and processes, and ensure transparency for ALL residents—not just a select few. We still need to: ✅ Implement a clear budget process to prevent financial mistakes. ✅ Strengthen coordination and communication between departments which is a challenge without anyone identified to take lead for Oakham. ✅ Stop the reliance on using free cash and our savings account for operations and focus on sustainable budgeting, which will require outside the box thinking. ✅ Modernize town governance so these same issues aren’t still being discussed in another 15 years. ✅ Fighting for financial support from the State of Massachusetts, while securing grants that are meant for rural communities. ✅ Identification of regional municipal collaboration opportunities (where appropriate), to continue our vital community services, without the financial burdens of doing it alone. Our budget likely can’t sustain the same level of these essential services going forward without significant cuts that impact safety, security and other supports to our residents. It’s time to break the cycle of limited action, driven by resistance to any change, lies, or fear mongering - that somehow improving our our town’s transparency or promoting the adoption of 21st century technology, financial policies or processes will change our rural character. If we do nothing to address today’s challenges, that is the biggest risk to our community. It’s time to look forward, not backwards when it comes to defending our rural way of life. I bring decades of successful business financial and operations leadership experience supporting teams of 5 to 200 staff and budgets of $2-$24 Million Dollars. Responsible for driving positive results in trying and prosperous situations. With my business acumen and my lifetime of service to youth and seniors and the past 12 years of elected and appointed service to Oakham, I am more than ready to bring the increased transparency, stability, and responsible financial strategic leadership to our community. I am ready to partner with our Board of Selectmen, all our Boards, Committees, and my fellow taxpayers on these old and new challenges. You have a choice, stay with the same approaches and challenges of 2009 or embrace the required approaches in 2025, I ask for your vote in May! For the 2009 and 2024 reports completed by the Commonwealth of Massachusetts please see my website for those and other sources of information. http://aaronlanglois.com']

Name: 98_clear mountain_mountain energy_madison_mountain
Representation: ['clear mountain', 'mountain energy', 'madison', 'mountain', 'madison fields', 'clermont county', 'energy center', 'clermont', 'madison county', 'agricultural approaches']
Representative_Docs: ['For generations, Clermont County farmers have fed and fueled the nation, and now, Clermont County has the chance to harvest a stable cash crop – the sun. Landowners choose to make their land available for solar, and it’s not a choice they make lightly. As stewards of the land, farmers who choose to participate in solar projects do so knowing that it’s a temporary, reversible use of land that is compatible with agriculture, even during the project’s life. For Clear Mountain Energy Center, we are considering a mix of agricultural approaches across the site as well as the establishment of permanent vegetation that helps support local pollinators and bolster biodiversity. 🦋 Sign a letter of support – it takes less than 5 minutes:', 'Supporting private property rights means supporting landowners who make their land available for solar. For some landowners, hosting a solar project on part of their land can be a hedge against shifting commodity prices that can sustain the rest of their agricultural production. The Clear Mountain Energy Center team is considering a mix of agricultural approaches across the site as well as the establishment of permanent vegetation to help support local pollinators and bolster biodiversity. Plus, solar developments are temporary – at the end of a project’s life, the equipment is removed, and the land can be returned to farming. Sign a letter of support TODAY if you stand for landowners’ right to make decisions for their property.', "Some of the best parts about utility-scale agrivoltaics are the ability to combine multiple agricultural approaches on the same site and plant the seeds for success on future projects! 🪻 In addition to grazing, forage, cover crops, and row crops on Madison Fields Solar Project, our developer’s operational project in Madison County, we've planted 25+ acres of restored Ohio prairie. Tailoring our approach to the local community and ecosystem is essential, and our work on Madison Fields will inform our approach on Clear Mountain. In Madison County, we proudly collaborated with Madison Soil and Water Conservation District @[100064746546862:2048:Madison Soil and Water Conservation District] and Columbus.Metro.Parks to harvest seed pods from the Darby Creek watershed, which were donated by the County to establish this prairie restoration area."]

Grab Yours🔗 >> https://t.site/4jwfZSP', '👕 Make Your Voice Heard: Tired of endless online debates? Show your commitment to real change with our bold statement tee! Representative_Docs: ['Virginia is undergoing a massive economic, technological, and environmental transformation, all centered around the activities of one industry: data centers. The explosive growth of this energy-intensive sector — the only growing sector of electricity demand in Virginia — is requiring historic expansions of our energy grid and will thwart our ability to get off of fossil fuels.⛽ And it will all be funded by Virginia ratepayers like yourself. This unprecedented development will erase hard-won conservation and climate gains in Virginia, on top of the local impacts to land, water, quality of life, climate, habitat and air and noise pollution. Get involved locally with PEC in Charlottesville and Albemarle as we advocate for a more transparent and fair data center industry. Learn more at https://pecva.org/datacenters 🔌 📈 #datacenters #ai #artificialintellegence #climatechange #energy #renewableenergy #virginia #cleanwater #cleanair #albemarle #charlottesville', 'Virginia is home to over 300 data centers with more on the way. Just one of these facilities can use as much electricity as a city of 150,000 people. As a result, Virginia’s energy demand is skyrocketing. We need to make sure the data center industry pays for the energy costs tied to their growth, not Virginia families. Support the Fair Share Energy Act.,Virginia is home to over 300 data centers with more on the way. Just one of these facilities can use as much electricity as a city of 150,000 people. As a result, Virginia’s energy demand is skyrocketing. We need to make sure the data center industry pays for the energy costs tied to their growth, not Virginia families. Support the Fair Share Energy Act.,Virginia is home to over 300 data centers with more on the way. Just one of these facilities can use as much electricity as a city of 150,000 people. As a result, Virginia’s energy demand is skyrocketing. We need to make sure the data center industry pays for the energy costs tied to their growth, not Virginia families. Support the Fair Share Energy Act.', '⚡ Data centers are the only growing sector of electricity demand in Virginia, growth that is projected to more than double peak load in 15 years – meaning that data centers alone could double Virginia’s electricity consumption. Instead of working towards our renewable energy goals, Virginia is taking a step backward. PEC is working at the state and local levels to enhance transparency within the industry, establish state-level oversight, provide protections for families and businesses and incentivize best practices that reduce pollution. In Albemarle, we’ve already helped put limits on larger data centers being built without legislative review. Now, we need your help making sure the Data Center Ordinance protects the community from any potential negative impacts. Learn more at pecva.org/datacenters 🔌 📈 #datacenters #ai #artificialintellegence #climatechange #energy #renewableenergy #virginia #cleanwater #cleanair #albemarle #charlottesville']

They\'re unnatural 🏭 Join hundreds in swapping planet-overheating fossil fuels for clean energy.', 'Congress repeals oil and gas methane pollution fee, costing taxpayers $7.2 billion One of only three bills the Republican-controlled Congress has passed so far in 2025 is a measure repealing a fee on excessive emissions of methane. On Feb. 27, Congress passed legislation killing a charge on waste methane emissions from the oil and gas industry. While the Trump Administration fires thousands of federal workers, allegedly to save taxpayer money, eliminating the pollution control fee will cost taxpayers $7.2 billion in uncollected revenue from the oil & gas industry over the next decade, according to the Congressional Budget Office. Methane is a potent greenhouse gas, with 30 times the potential to warm the atmosphere as carbon dioxide. Methane is responsible for around 30 percent of the rise in global temperatures since the Industrial Revolution, according to the International Energy Agency. Oil and natural gas systems are the largest source of methane in the U.S., accounting for 28 percent of emissions, according to the EPA. Photo: A natural gas drilling site in Westmoreland County, Pennsylvania. Photo by Ted Auch, FracTracker Alliance, 2021.']

Name: 102_alaska_alaska energy_alaskans_railbelt
Representative_Docs: ["🌊🔌 Big News for Alaska's Energy Future! Alaska is set to receive a $207 million grant from the U.S. Department of Energy to fund a crucial undersea power cable across Cook Inlet. This cable will connect the Kenai Peninsula’s power infrastructure to the Railbelt grid, running roughly from Nikiski to Beluga, and create a more resilient and efficient energy network for the region. ⚡ By improving energy transmission from the Kenai Peninsula, this project will bolster Alaska's power supply, allowing more renewable energy from the Bradley Lake Hydroelectric Project, near Homer, to be distributed throughout the Railbelt system. It will also support the transmission of power from future renewable projects on the Kenai Peninsula to interior Alaska. This is a win for Alaska’s energy security and sustainability! 🌍 To learn more about this and other federal infrastructure funding impacting Alaska, check out our map at BuildAlaska.org! 📸: Alaska Division of Community and Regional Affairs #CookInletCable #EnergyEfficiency #DOEGrant #BuildBackAK #AlaskaInfrastructure", 'A Gateway to Opportunity: What the federal investment in Port of Nome means to all of Alaska Last week, we talked about the economic impacts of the Port of Nome Expansion Project. Today we want to talk about how this project is set to create nearly 3,000 jobs statewide, with more than 800 new jobs in Nome—a crucial boost for our local economy! 🔹 Construction Jobs: A wealth of opportunities will arise for engineers, operators, electricians, and skilled tradespeople during the construction phase. 🔹 Long-Term Employment: The completed port will offer sustainable roles in operations, maintenance, and administration. 🔹 Indirect Job Market Impacts: This expansion will stimulate indirect job growth in various sectors like tourism, hospitality, cargo, and natural resources, benefiting the entire region. 🔹 Workforce Development: Training initiatives will prepare local residents for emerging opportunities, fostering a skilled workforce for the future. For more on the Port of Nome and other impactful projects that are shaping Alaska’s future, check out our interactive map at buildAlaska.org - see firsthand how these investments are making a difference in local communities! Additionally, if you’ve been following our work, please consider contributing to Build Alaska’s Future Education Fund through Pick. Click. Give. when you file for your PFD at PFD.Alaska.gov! @[100068904022491:2048:City of Nome] @[100064638671460:2048:Alaska District, U.S. Army Corps of Engineers] #PortofNome #BuildAlaska #FederalInvestments #EconomicGrowth', 'I’m All In For Affordable, Reliable, Sustainable, and Renewable Energy for Alaska! An area of consensus among the Interior Delegation of state legislators is the need to lower the high cost of energy for the Interior. Reducing the cost of energy will make our economy stronger and make Fairbanks more affordable for working families. The last legislative session was nicknamed the energy session, with the legislature passing several important bills and making strategic investments to make energy along the Railbelt more affordable, reliable, sustainable, and renewable. Here are some of the important bills and investments that passed and that I supported: ✅ $32 million in matching funds for the Grid Resilience and Innovation Partnership (GRIP) Grant. This is $206.5 million federal grant that was secured by Congresswoman Mary Pelotla and our congressional delegation last year to make needed improvements to the railbelt. This match is just a start as the State of Alaska is required to provide a one to one match of the whole $206.5 million. It is critical that we provide all required matching funds so we can leverage this whole grant, and get future grants. I will continue advocating for additional matching funds. ✅ HB 307 creates a Railbelt Transmission Organization (RTO) to make the railbelt more efficient and reliable. This will help eliminate what experts in the field call “pancake” rates by stabilizing prices and preventing pooling along the grid. I proudly voted for this bill and am pleased that it became law earlier this year. ✅ SB 152 establishes a legal framework for community energy facilities including community solar, allowing ratepayers to pool their resources together to bring clean and affordable energy to their communities. I proudly co-sponsored this bill and am pleased that it became law this summer. ✅ HB62 makes the Renewable Energy Grant Fund permanent. Since its creation it has distributed $275 million in grants for renewable energy projects, and will no longer be subject to continual re-authorization. ✅ $2.5 million for the Interior Gas Utility to build out our natural gas network in Fairbanks and hook more homes up to cheaper and cleaner burning natural gas. ✅ Supporting the Shovel Creek Windfarm. In January, I submitted a letter of support for the leases for the Shovel Creek Wind Farm on the north side of Murphy Dome. This wind farm will become operational by 2027 and will create over 200 megawatts of energy, putting downward pressure on our energy rates and adding a major source of renewable power to our grid. I hope to continue perusing policies and investments to make energy more affordable in Fairbanks. I would be honored to have your vote on or before November 5. #ashleyforalaska #allinforak #akleg #akelect']

Tell your State Leader: Keep supporting homegrown energy because Ohio wins with clean power!', 'מרססים לנו את השמים עם חומרים כימיים מסוכנים כגון מתכות כבדות, פוגעים לנו בבריאות, מזהמים לנו את המים, מרעילים לנו את המזון. אלו הן עובדות, זאת מציאות אמיתית ומסוכנת שאי אפשר יותר להתכחש לה או להתעלם ממנה. הכל נמצא מול הפנים, מתועד, ממוסמך ופוסט הזה אני אציג לפניכם את ההוכחות . פוסט מעט ארוך אך ממצא ומכיל את מרבית החומרים הקיימים הזמינים בעת הזאת אז הקדישו מעט מזמנכם היקר ופשוט פתחו את העיניים, הפעילו את מוחכם, הביטו סביב והבינו באיזו סביבה אנו חיים ומה עובר עלינו . ובכדי שמידע זה יגיע לכל בית אפשרי , מומלץ להגיב ולשתף ברבים . חיינו וחיי משפחותינו יכול להיות תלוי הזה, יכול עוד להינצל . לא משנה כמה תיעודים, הוכחות, עדויות תפרסם על פעולות הנדסת האקלים ובמיוחד על ריסוסי הכמיכלים בשמיים עדיין יהיו כאלו שיטענו כי אלו פסי התעבות ולא פסי כמיכלים מסוכנים מסוכנים שמרוססים בשמיים. אבל מזל שיש לנו את האמריקנים, בעיקר את המדינות הרפובליקניות בהנהגה ימנית אמיתית, שמציגות לנו את האמת בפנים . \u200fבסוף כל ״הקונספירציות״ שנטען שהן תאוריות , מתבררות כאמת . \u200fבאמת מהנדסים את האקלים , באמצעים שונים , גם בארץ. \u200fואנו רואים זאת יום יום בשמי הארץ. \u200fעכשיו איווה ופלורידה מצטרפות למדינות שמקדמות חוק איסור הנדסת אקלים \u200fאחרי קנטאקי ואריזונה. \u200fהתחילו עכשיו באיווה להעביר חוק האוסר הנדסת מזג האוויר. \u200fהטכנולוגיה שואפת להוריד זמנית את הטמפרטורה וגם לרצוח אותנו על הדרך. \u200fאיווה תאסור פליטת מזהמים לאוויר במטרה לשנות את מזג האוויר. \u200fהעונש צפוי להיות עד 5 שנות מאסר וקנס. \u200fבפלורידה, הצעה דומה עברה בסנאט לפני מספר ימים . \u200fIowa joins GOP-led states in advancing anti-\'chemtrails\' bill banning geoengineering https://www.desmoinesregister.com/story/news/politics/2025/02/12/anti-chemtrails-bill-in-iowa-house-would-ban-geoengineering/78426920007/ Others would call it conspiracy theories\': Florida anti-chemtrail bill advances. What that means What are chemtrails? Conspiracy theory has persisted for decades https://www.tallahassee.com/story/news/politics/2025/02/12/florida-bill-chemtrails-weather-modification-sb-56/78459183007/ \u200fלא רק שזה לא תיאורית קונספירציה, זו היא קונספירציה הלכה למעשה . \u200fהמילה קונספירציה באה מהמילה Conspiracy, קשר . \u200fואם נתרגם To Conspire, נבין שזה אומר קשירת קשר . \u200fויש אפילו על סעיף בדין הפלילי. \u200fיש בכל העולם , בכל רגע נתון, מאות ואף אלפי פעולות שמתבצעים בהן פעולות של הנדסת אקלים . \u200fותוכלו לראות במה הזאת שמתעדכן און ליין . \u200f\u2066\u202amap.geoengineeringmonitor.org\u202c\u2069 \u200fגם בארצו הקטנטונת והסביבה הקרובה מתנהלים מספר פעולות של הנדסת אקלים . \u200eנתחיל מהצפון : \u200eIsrael Rain Enhancement Project \u200eDescription \u200eThis cloud seeding program was carried out on experimental scale from 1960 to 1975. After 1975, the program entered the operational phase and cloud seeding was carried out in northern Israel. In January 2021, Israel discontinued the rain enhancement project because of too little effectiveness and too high costs https://map.geoengineeringmonitor.org/wm/israel-rain-enhancement-project \u200fחברה נוספת מתכננת לשחרר לתוך הים, היכן שהדגים שאנו אוכלים נמצאים , בסוג של הפריית האוקיינוס, חלקיקים מועשרים בחומרים מזינים. \u200fשבטח אנו לא נדע מה הם מכילים . \u200fהחברה נקראת \u200fGigaBlue Ltd https://map.geoengineeringmonitor.org/ggr/gigablue-ltd \u200eחברה נוספת היא \u200eRewind: Sea of Galilee \u200eScale \u200eThe company plans to remove atmospheric CO2 by dumping agricultural and forestry biomass on the seabed of the Black Sea in anoxic areas, i.e. areas depleted of dissolved oxygen. Field trials in the Sea of Galilee with different types of biomass, for different periods of time, to test decomposition rates, including plant biomass and hardwood https://map.geoengineeringmonitor.org/other/rewind-sea-of-galilee חברה נוספת RepAir Carbon Capture Scale Developer of DAC technology. Small-scale CO2 capture. Prototype since 11/2023. No information available on the CO2 capture capacity of the prototype. According to RepAir the technology requires 0.6 MWh to capture 1t of CO2. The company aims to produce "carbon removal credits of the highest quality". RepAir promises "carbon removal at the gigaton scale". https://map.geoengineeringmonitor.org/ggr/repair-carbon-capture חברה נוספת Carbon Blue Ltd The company aims to capture CO2 from seawater. R&D, laboratory scale testing, trials in a seawater pool. https://map.geoengineeringmonitor.org/ggr/carbon-blue-ltd \u200fוהדובדבן של הקצפת , מה שאנו טוענים כבר שנים שעושים לנו , שמרססים את השמים שלנו בלי הפסקה, וכלי התשקורת והממסד כל הזמן ניסו לספר סיפורים ושקרים בנוגע למה שאנו רואים ומרגישים . \u200fבארץ מתקיים ניסוי האלק, של חברת Stardust Solutions \u200fשהוקמה על ידי ידוב ינאי, ספקטור עמיעד \u200eוהדוח שלהם : \u200eSolar radiation modification \u200ePublication metadata \u200eSolar radiation modification’ is an umbrella term for proposed technologies that would reflect more sunlight back into space, or allow more infrared radiation to escape into space, thereby creating a net cooling effect on the Earth’s climate. This scientific opinion by the Group of Chief Scientific Advisors (GCSA) examines how the EU can address the risks and opportunities associated with research on solar radiation modification and with its potential deployment. It also presents the possible options for a governance system for research and potential deployment, considering different solar radiation modification technologies and their scale. This opinion is published in the context of the Scientific Advice Mechanism (SAM) which provides independent scientific evidence and policy recommendations to the European institutions by request of the College of Commissioners. https://op.europa.eu/o/opportal-service/download-handler?identifier=9c2ac367-b5de-11ef-acb1-01aa75ed71a1&format=pdf&language=en&productionSystem=cellar&part= \u200fבדו״ח הם כותבים כך: \u200fאסטרטגיה מסוכנת \u200fזו אסטרטגיה מסוכנת. SRM טומן בחובו אי ודאויות גדולות וסיכונים רחבי טווח שאינם מובנים היטב. \u200fהפריסה "ככל הנראה תביא להשפעות אקולוגיות וכלכליות שליליות משמעותיות, כולל שינוי דפוסי גשמים, השפעות על מערכות אקולוגיות, ירידה באבטחת ייצור המזון ובפוטנציאל של אנרגיית השמש", מזהיר דו"ח האיחוד האירופי. \u200fRisky strategy \u200fIt’s a risky strategy. SRM carries major uncertainties and wide-ranging risks that are poorly understood. \u200fDeployment is “likely to bring substantial negative ecological and economic effects, including changing patterns of rainfall, impacts on ecosystems, a decrease in the security of food production and in the potential of solar energy,” the EU report warns. \u200eב 28/02/2023 \u200eהתפרסם על דו״ח של האו״ם \u200eNew report explores issues around solar radiation modification \u200eעל הבעתיות של SRM \u200eEarlier this week, the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) released a report that examines the status of research along with the potential risks and impacts of solar radiation modification(SRM), a group of technologies that aim to cool the planet. https://www.unep.org/news-and-stories/story/new-report-explores-issues-around-solar-radiation-modification הדוח : One Atmosphere: An Independent Expert Review on Solar Radiation Modification Research and Deployment https://wedocs.unep.org/bitstream/handle/20.500.11822/41903/one_atmosphere.pdf?sequence=3&isAllowed=y \u200eעוד מהדו״ח : \u200eWhat is SRM and why is UNEP concerned about the technologies? \u200eAH: SRM includes technologies to cool the planet, the most mature of which is stratospheric aerosol injection. This involves injecting aerosols into the stratosphere, so a small amount of sunlight is deliberately reflected into space to cool the planet. This process is similar to what happens when volcanoes erupt and aerosols are pushed into the stratosphere, with measurable cooling following. \u200eWhile there is a growing body of literature and advanced modelling on the topic, which has been developing for many years, there is very little evidence or research on the risks and impacts of SRM. The report outlines these limitations in the evidence base. UNEP is concerned that while the scientific literature shows that SRM, and stratospheric aerosol injection specifically, can cool the globe, there is a dearth of data on the risks and impacts. For technologies that can alter the chemistry of the stratosphere, the world needs much more information to support decision-making. \u200eWhy is SRM so controversial? \u200eAH: A range of concerns about SRM were raised in the report and included the scenario that if SRM were to be unilaterally deployed by a rogue state or non-state actor, like a private company, it could introduce a series of new complex geo-political or security threats.
