Name: 123_salmonstate_bycatch_trawl_fishermen
Representative_Docs: ["We were thrilled when, this summer, a three-judge panel at the 9th U.S. Circuit Court of appeals overturned a decision that could have shut down Southeast Alaska’s commercial salmon troll fishery — a sustainable small boat, hook-and-line fishery that catches salmon one at a time. The Wild Fish Conservancy, a Washington state-based group, filed the lawsuit in 2020, alleging that trollers take prey from the Southern Resident Killer Whale population of the Salish Sea. Later this year, a new scientific study showed that in the summer, those killer whales actually have more king salmon available to them than the growing Northern Resident Killer Whale population of coastal British Columbia and Southeast Alaska — making even more clear just how misguided this lawsuit was. Photo: The F/V Sakura, captained by Tad Fujioka, a Sitka-based troller who passed away unexpectedly this year. Tad fought hard for Southeast Alaska's sustainable fisheries, for trollers, and against this misguided lawsuit.at, hook-and-line fishery that catches salmon one at a time. of trollers; drove grassroots action, and created several videos and other materials making sure people were aware of the issue. We are proud to stand with Southeast Alaska fishermen and trollers to fight this lawsuit, and we will continue to do so should another threat arise to this sustainable fishery, which has roots in virtually every Southeast Alaska community. Photo: The F/V Sakura, captained by Tad Fujioka, a Sitka-based troller who passed away unexpectedly this year. Tad fought hard for Southeast Alaska sustainable fisheries, for trollers, and against this misguided lawsuit.", "“I think the biggest concern is we're not gonna have salmon anymore. I mean, the global warming — the temperature has to be right in the river for them to come up to lay their eggs. It’s too warm for them. And the overfishing down in the ocean — I really think that one has a big impact on how much fish is coming back to the river. Our younger generation — take care of the planet. Take care of it. That's all I can say. Learn what's good and what's not. Fight for it.” —Wanda Deacon, Grayling, Alaska Story 4 of 4 from Wanda Deacon While Yukon River peoples forgo traditional ways of life to conserve wild salmon, trawlers continue with business as usual — bycatching a documented 141 million pounds of marine life on average each year. This bycatch includes king salmon, chum salmon, halibut, herring, squid, crab, whales and more. Trawlers trail nets the size of football fields to catch everything in the water column. When they drag the bottom, they rip up seafloor habitat and crush bottom-dwelling species in a way that never comes up to the surface to be counted. And “midwater” trawlers, which are allowed to trawl in protected areas, drag the bottom up to 100% of the time. Right now, they’re allowed to bycatch an unlimited number of chum salmon. Take action to tell the North Pacific Fishery Management Council this needs to change: salmonstate.org/bycatch #stoptrawlbycatch #subsistence #alaska #wildsalmon #YukonRiver #GraylingAlaska Photos by Jacqueline Cleveland", 'As America’s largest fishery, U.S. pollock leads the world in science-based management and marine mammal protections. It’s a trawl fishery, with the majority harvested in federal waters off Alaska in the Bering Sea — waters that belong to every American. NOAA recently completed a sweeping review of 2,500 fisheries in 135 nations. The verdict? 240 fisheries from 46 countries failed to meet the test — and will be barred from the U.S. market beginning in 2026. Meanwhile, the U.S. pollock catcher vessels represented by United Catcher Boats already meet the highest bar: strict 100% monitoring, proven safeguards, and U.S. law that prohibits intentionally harming marine mammals. Like every fishery in the world, incidental bycatch does occur — but in the U.S. pollock fleet, it is tracked, managed, and reduced through science-based tools that set the global benchmark. That’s something every American can be proud of. That’s why attacks on U.S. seafood — and especially the trawl sector that harvests pollock, the largest food fishery in the nation — are more than local politics. They’re attacks on: National security – weakening our domestic food supply. Food equity – threatening access to affordable protein for American families. Global leadership – if the U.S. doesn’t set the standard for sustainable seafood, who will fill the void? Russia? Venezuela? Nations with no comparability findings at all? Choosing to support Alaska pollock means backing a fleet that leads with science, manages bycatch responsibly, protects marine mammals, and delivers food security. Read NOAA’s findings: https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/international-affairs/2025-marine-mammal-protection-act-comparability-finding-determinations United Catcher Boats — Proud to fish for America. Proud to set the standard.']

I am dedicated to preserving these vital areas, ensuring they remain accessible to all, and advocating for sustainable practices that enhance our quality of life and protect our environment for future generations.” 🔥 With Casey at the helm, our town’s natural beauty will be protected, our air will be cleaner, and our future will be brighter! Let’s come together to fight for our green spaces and make Mount Holly a shining example of sustainability! 🌱🌿 Ready for change? Let’s make it happen together!🙌 #LCVEndorsement #MountHollyUnited 🌍 #EcoWarrior #GreenSpacesMatter 🌳 #SustainableFuture #VoteForCasey #ProtectWhatMatters #CleanAirCleanWater 💧 #EnvironmentalJustice #VoteForThePlanet #VoteOnClimate #NJElections #ClimateActionNow #ClimateCantWait #CleanWater #CleanEnergy #ElectionsMatter #CleanEnergyForAll', '🌿🌎 BIG NEWS! 🌟 We are thrilled to announce that Bjana Swinson has officially been endorsed by the League of Conservation Voters! 🙌💚 In Bjana’s words: ”I am honored to accept the endorsement of the League of Conservation Voters. Their work in protecting our environment aligns with my vision for a greener, healthier Mount Holly. This partnership reflects our shared commitment to clean air, safe drinking water, and preserving natural spaces for future generations. Together, we will continue to push for policies that prioritize sustainability and environmental justice, ensuring that Mount Holly remains a vibrant and eco-friendly community for all.” This endorsement is a testament to Bjana’s dedication to building a sustainable future for Mount Holly! 🌿💧 Let’s continue to fight for clean air, safe water, and a greener tomorrow! 🌱✨ #MountHollyUnited #EnvironmentalJustice #GoGreen 🌍 #LCVEndorsement #CleanAirAndWater #EcoFriendlyFuture 🌿 #SustainabilityMatters #VoteForChange', 'BIG NEWS and thanks @[100064439182071:2048:NH Sierra Club Chapter] for your environmental and energy endorsement! NH SIERRA CLUB ENDORSES REBECCA MCWILLIAMS CONCORD, NH: Today, the New Hampshire Chapter of Sierra Club announced its endorsement of Rebecca McWilliams of Concord in the State Senate District 15 primary election. State Senate District 15 includes the towns of Bow, Concord, and Hopkinton. “We are pleased to announce today that the Sierra Club officially endorses Rebecca McWilliams for election to New Hampshire’s State Senate District 15,” said Laura Magzis, NH Sierra Club Political Team Member. “We are confident that she will continue work to protect New Hampshire families’ health, air and water, and build a clean energy economy that will lower energy bills, create new jobs, improve public health, and expand clean energy options for all Granite Staters.” Honorable Representative Lee Oxenham, Plainfield, Sullivan County District 1 and NHSC Chapter Executive Committee Member: "As a former colleague on the House Science, Technology, and Energy Committee, I witnessed firsthand Rebecca’s keen intellect at redesigning building codes, supporting access to clean energy for all Granite Staters, and protecting our state’s natural resources. Her passion, empathy, and legal dexterity have won her the admiration of legislators on both sides of the aisle and outstanding record of success in the State House. As a NH Sierra Club Executive Committee Member and a former legislator, I am excited to support her as the next NH State Senator for Concord, Bow, and Hopkinton." Arthur B. Cunningham, Attorney in Hopkinton, NHSC Executive and Political Committee Member: "While the Chapter endorses Rebecca McWilliams for many reasons, I personally witnessed her strong support for New Hampshire electric ratepayers as it relates to energy costs. After the skyrocketing electric rates of 2022, she listened to her constituents suffering from the unprecedented energy bills. She was unafraid to pursue the root cause of high energy bills by holding utilities and the state accountable for lack of oversight and ignoring their own process. Future Senator McWilliams will demand that utilities, with their privileged economic power over ratepayers and state agencies with the authority to curb utility abuse, act lawfully ultimately protecting New Hampshire’s clean air, clean water, and natural treasures. Jerry Curran, NH Chapter Chair: "Rebecca McWilliams is a strong advocate for shifting away from polluting coal to more efficient and cost effective energy that will not only lower carbon and mercury emissions but continue to champion lower energy bills and local job creation. After the hottest July in a generation, New Hampshire has some serious work to do and Rebecca McWilliams understands the assignment. She will not disappoint as the District 15 State Senator. Along with the endorsement, the NH Sierra Club will lend its volunteer strength to McWilliams’ campaign." "We pledge to do all we can to help ensure Rebecca McWilliams is elected," said Catherine Corkery, NH Chapter Director. "Scores of Chapter volunteers will contact district voters on her behalf and we will speak as often as possible about her exemplary environmental record. We look forward to a victory for McWilliams on September 10th and to McWilliams fighting for the environment as the next N.H. State Senator for District 15." Rebecca McWilliams Background As a working architect and attorney, Rebecca is an expert advocate with over 20 years experience managing construction projects, addressing risk, and negotiating contracts. As owner of the Lewis Farm with her husband James, she understands the needs of the NH business community. Rebecca and James are the parents of two children who attend public schools, and a child in childcare. Rebecca was elected for three terms as the House representative in Concord. Statement from Candidate Rebecca McWilliams, D-Concord: "Now is the time to act on climate. Don\'t sit this one out. Over the past six years, I\'ve worked with the NH Sierra Club on net metering, vehicle air emissions, funding weatherization projects, streamlining renewable interconnection schedules, our state energy code, stabilizing electricity rates, and shutting down the Merrimack Station coal plant. We are building on that success together to fight to hold PFAs polluters accountable and end PFAs pollution in the NH environment. We will win this together. While in the minority, I’ve continued to push for energy and environmental bills (filing 26 bills related to energy and environment) that will move the state towards the future including: increasing availability of zero emissions vehicles on NH car dealer lots and addressing air quality (2020 HB1444, 2021 HB168, 2022 HB92), addressing fair electric rates for ratepayers with portfolio diversity (2023 HB159 with the Sierra Club), creating a clean energy accelerator fund (2022 HB1506), requiring construction demolition debris be recycled (2022 HB1544), and increasing the renewable energy net metering cap for all (2024 HB1600, 2023 HB523, 2022 HB1596, 2021 HB167, 2020 HB1218, and 2019 HB365). Finally, this race is crucial to the future of landfills and PFAs in NH!! We will have several key votes in the next two years regarding whether we will expand landfills to take on more out-of-state waste, and if we will finally stand up to serial polluters to prevent PFAs, forever chemicals, in our soil, air and water – with exorbitant costs for cleanup and cancer. This Senate Seat is CRUCIAL to determine the next steps our state will take for everyone!!!" Rebecca understands the assignment. She is committed to continuing to champion NH’s energy and environmental future while working in the Senate. She can\'t do it alone, so join us at www.rebeccamcwilliams.com to volunteer and donate.']

Name: 126_insurance_insurance rates_insurance companies_insurers
Representation: ['insurance', 'insurance rates', 'insurance companies', 'insurers', 'rising insurance', 'insurance costs', 'homeowners insurance', 'premiums', 'ceos', 'florida house']
Representative_Docs: ['In January 2025, wildfires in Los Angeles caused significant destruction, destroying over 12,000 buildings and resulting in at least 24 fatalities. ￼ Prior to these events, several insurance companies, including State Farm, Chubb Insurance, Allstate, Tokio Marine America Insurance Co., and Trans Pacific Insurance Co., ceased renewing homeowners’ insurance policies in high-risk fire areas like Pacific Palisades. This decision was influenced by stringent California regulations that limited insurers’ ability to adjust premiums in response to increasing risks, particularly those associated with climate change. In response to the escalating crisis, California implemented significant insurance reforms in December 2024, marking the most substantial changes in three decades. These reforms aimed to stabilize the market and encourage insurers to continue providing coverage within the state. Despite these efforts, the devastating wildfires in Los Angeles in January 2025 exacerbated the situation, leading to increased scrutiny of insurance companies like State Farm. However, it is crucial to recognize that the primary causes of the insurance crisis are multifaceted, encompassing regulatory challenges and environmental factors, rather than the actions of individual insurers alone. Public figures, such as Tyler Perry, have openly criticized insurance companies for canceling policies before the fires, labeling it as “pure greed.” ￼ This situation underscores the need for a balanced approach to insurance regulation that considers both consumer interests and the financial stability of insurers amid escalating environmental risks.', "Condo fees have doubled because insurance rates have doubled I always want to be informed on the issues that matter to homeowners in District 100… On Sunday, September 15, I attended a forum focused on the skyrocketing insurance rates hitting condo owners in coastal areas. It was front page news in the Sun Sentinel. Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse - Rhode Island, joined South Florida Rep. Debbie Wasserman Schultz and Rep. Jared Moskowitz along with local officials on Sunday afternoon at Hollywood City Hall. Our rising Insurance costs are directly tied to climate change. This is not just a Florida issue—it's a national crisis. Senator Whitehouse explained, “If insurers can’t predict damage from climate risks, they can’t insure properties. An uninsurable property is an unmortgage-able property...The next big shock to the US economy will be climate-driven.” Rep. Debbie Wasserman Schultz added: “The skyrocketing cost of insurance is one of the biggest threats to keeping people in their homes.” So, what can we do? 1. Elect leaders who believe in climate change and will fight for common-sense solutions to protect homeowners. 2. Vote for someone who will fight for YOU, not the insurance companies, and will bring real solutions to this crisis. I will stand up for homeowners and work for real solutions to this growing threat. Have questions? I am available to talk! #JimBrennerforStateHouse #CondoInsuranceCrisis #ClimateChange #InsuranceSouthFlorida #VoteSmart", 'We are blessed to live in a beautiful house on a beautiful creek in Englewood, Florida. At least we did until Helene roared through and sent more than two feet of Lemon Bay into our living room. Lots of you are in this same situation, staring at some hard work ahead. This is our home and we will restore it It is just one more obstacle to overcome. When I decided to run for Florida House District 75, I pledged to fight to make insurance affordable again. That goal remains, but what a lot of people are thinking right now is, “Hey mister, pay my claim!” Of course, my insurance company has put us up at the Hampton Inn and is sending us another check in the morning. Right! Just kidding! Having the patience of Job while hoping to get an insurance claim settled is also one more obstacle to overcome. It helps to have good friends, relatives and neighbors to lend helping hands when you’re down. Here in Englewood and Grove City, Placida and South Venice, Rotonda and Port Charlotte, there are lots of people like that. They care about each other, and their generosity of spirit in times like these is humbling. While storm recovery is again on many people’s minds, the other issues in my campaign haven’t gone away. Issues like clean water, controlled development, protecting women’s rights, local control and affordable housing. If you’ve been following my campaign you know where I stand on each of these issues, and if you want to know more you can go to my website https://tonydunbarfordistrict75.org/ We don’t need to be stuck in a rut here in Charlotte and Sarasota Counties, watching the bulldozers flatten more woodland for more subdivisions – watching our streets flood every time there’s big rain. We can actually start to fix things. I have no personal issue with my opponent, Danny Nix, I’m sure he is a fine man. But I do have a problem with his campaign. His treasurer is Eric Robinson, sometimes referred to as the “Prince of Dark Money.” Mr. Robinson has brought in money from almost 50 PACs to support my opponent’s campaign, representing every special interest imaginable, from HCA Hospitals to Comcast to Duke Energy. Most of these PACs, however, represent land developers and the construction industry. And insurance companies. All in all, they have dumped more than $400,000 into the Nix campaign. I am grateful for the more than 140 individuals (not PACs) who have sent me campaign contributions totaling more than $35,000. These are people who care about our future. When you cast your ballot, I urge you to think about what you are voting for. If your plan is for more of the same - to further enrich the subdivision developers and the insurance companies here in Southwest Florida, so be it. But I want you to know that you have a choice. We are going to rebuild our home; we could rebuild our community and its future. Beating these guys is another obstacle to overcome, maybe the biggest yet. I urge you to stand up for your community and our neighborhoods and vote for positive change. Together we can make it happen! Tony Dunbar Candidate for Florida House of Representatives District 75']

Name: 129_new mexico_mexico_nm_schools healthcare

Representative_Docs: ['New Mexico’s oil & gas industry: ✅ Creates thousands of jobs ✅ Funds nearly half of the state’s budget ✅ Keeps energy affordable for families Yet, extreme policies threaten to destroy jobs, raise costs, and cut funding for schools and healthcare. It’s time to stand up for New Mexico’s workers and families! Tell lawmakers: VOTE NO.', 'My district relies heavily on the Oil and Gas industry. The truth is that all of New Mexico does—after all, the industry pays for our public education system, as well as many other parts of the state budget. But for us, here, it’s an existential issue, as most of the economy in Southeast New Mexico depends on it. That’s why I wanted to make you all aware of some very bad bills for our industry currently making their way through the legislature. Please call or email your legislators, particularly Democrat legislators, and ask them to oppose these bills. SB 4 – NM Clean Air Act – sets unattainable standards and funds the program by relying on enforcement actions, thereby incentivizing enforcement SB 21 – NM Clean Water Act – goes well beyond the Federal Clean Water Act and amounts to massive overreach; also relies on fees and penalties to fund the program, thereby incentivizing further overreach SB 23 – Royalty rate increases for certain oil and gas leases – placing an undue burden on certain businesses SB 48 & SB 49 – Community Projects Fund directing funds to be spent on anti-oil/gas development. SB 178 – Produced water that would severely limit the use of produced water in NM HB 35 – Setbacks bill HB 257, HB 258, HB 259 – Oil and Gas Act changes', "Oil and Gas companies have been Breaking Bad, destroying our climate and flooding the airwaves with fear mongering ads. We have seen devastating climate catastrophes increasing in frequency and severity. But there is hope! Senate Bill 4, the Clear Horizons Act, is coming up for a hearing in the Senate Finance Committee and we need your help voicing support for this landmark piece of legislation! It's of crucial importance that the state of New Mexico prepare for the mounting climate crisis with common sense goals and plans to gradually reduce emissions. Click the link to contact representatives on Senate Finance and tell them to vote YES for SB4. Click the link in this post or in our bio. #newmexico #renewableenergy #greenenergy #climatechange #climatecrisis #breakingbad #nmlegis #nmstatelegislature #2025session"]

It's not just a gift; it's your impact.", "Ever watch a sea turtle glide through the ocean and feel your heart do a little flip? Maybe you're like me - completely captivated by these gentle giants who've been swimming our seas since the time of dinosaurs, carrying ancient wisdom in their eyes and entire ecosystems on their shells. There's something magical about them, isn't there? The way they navigate thousands of miles of ocean using invisible magnetic fields, how mother turtles return to the very same beach where they hatched to lay their own eggs, how their presence alone helps maintain the health of our oceans - keeping coral reefs thriving and sea grass beds healthy. But here's the thing about falling in love with sea turtles - it often comes with a heavy heart. Because while these incredible creatures have survived for over 100 million years, they're facing challenges bigger than ever before. Plastic pollution, habitat loss, climate change - sometimes it feels overwhelming to think about everything they're up against. That's exactly why we created this special collection. When you catch a glimpse of these designs on your wrist, they're more than just beautiful reminders of these amazing creatures - they're part of the solution. Each bracelet helps support Mote, one of the world's leading organizations in sea turtle conservation. Their groundbreaking research and rescue efforts are helping to ensure that future generations will still get to experience that heart-flip moment of watching a sea turtle glide by. Sometimes it might remind you of that special moment you first fell in love with these incredible animals. Other times, it might help you remember that even small actions - like choosing ocean-friendly products - can make a real difference in protecting them. If you've ever felt a special connection to these ancient mariners of the sea, I hope you'll join us. These little hugs for your wrist are here to remind you that while sea turtles face big challenges, they also have people like you in their corner - and together, we can help protect them for generations to come."]

Name: 135_global summit_strategy solidarity_solidarity 2025_glfafrica
Representative_Docs: ["On 17 September, join us for a global conference to find out how Africa can navigate the future of three shifting landscapes: forests and savannas, drylands, and freshwater and oceans.,Éliane Ubalijoro is a powerhouse in the world of sustainability and gender equity. As CEO of CIFOR-ICRAF, she’s shaping the future of global landscapes. Catch her at #GLFAfrica and be part of the conversation!,Prof. Youba Sokona, a leading figure in energy, environment, and sustainable development, will be at #GLFAfrica! With over 40 years of impactful work and a role in IPCC assessments, his insights are invaluable.,Charlette N'Guessan is revolutionizing data with AMINI’s AI-powered solutions for climate and economic resilience. Join us at #GLFAfrica to see how she’s shaping the future of tech in Africa.,Meet Betty Kibaara, Director of the Food Initiative at The Rockefeller Foundation! 🌍 Betty is transforming food systems across Africa, from expanding school feeding programs to pioneering frass fertilizers with black soldier flies.,Dr. Solange Bandiaky-Badji, Coordinator of the Rights and Resources Initiative, is speaking at #GLFAfrica! With a deep focus on Indigenous and local community rights, she’s a leading advocate for gender justice and tenure rights. Don’t miss her insights!,Elizabeth Maruma Mrema, UNEP Deputy Executive Director, is set to share her vision at #GLFAfrica! Discover how her groundbreaking work in environmental law is shaping global sustainability.,From green bonds to credit enhancement, Mary Njuguna’s work in capital markets is revolutionizing economic growth in Africa. Hear her speak at #GLFAfrica on the latest in sustainable finance!,Dr. Susan Chomba, renowned for her work on forest conservation and sustainable landscapes, is joining us at #GLFAfrica. Don’t miss her insights on advancing climate action and food systems transformation!", 'Mark your calendars! Join us for a truly exceptional 2024 Africa Think Tank Summit as we celebrate our 10th anniversary. This year\'s summit, themed "Evidence-Based Strategies for Sustainable Climate Financing in Africa," will focus on identifying and evaluating effective strategies to mobilize, channel, and manage climate finance, including the role of carbon markets, for climate change mitigation and adaptation efforts. The event will explore key topics such as innovative financing instruments, strengthening technical and institutional capacity, successful initiatives, and policy frameworks that can promote sustainable climate investments and overcome barriers to climate finance access in Africa. Carbon markets in the context of African countries will also be an important topic for discussion. Through interactive side-events, workshops, panel discussions, and networking sessions, experts and thought leaders will share their insights and expertise, providing a comprehensive understanding of the challenges, opportunities, and best practices in ensuring effective and equitable utilization of climate finance. Don’t miss out! Join us in Abidjan, Cote d’Ivoire from October 8-10,2024, as we contribute to providing evidence-based innovative solutions for sustainable climate finance in Africa. Get a glimpse of the summit’s exciting programming and reserve your spot by September 13, 2024, using the registration link: https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/Q7MLTJF', "Dear All Governments and Health Systems, I am writing to share my understanding of the powerful connection between our thoughts, feelings, and the world around us. This letter is a culmination of our collaborative efforts, gathering evidence and wisdom from various teachers and sources. Our mission is to empower humanity to work together as one consciousness, recognizing that our thoughts, feelings, and actions have a profound impact on the world and the universe. The Vision My company is dedicated to facilitating a global awakening, promoting healing, education, and well-being on all levels. We aim to attract tourism, create jobs, and stimulate economic growth in various industries, including healthcare, education, music, film, and radio. Our ultimate goal is to raise the frequency of the earth, stop war, and bring better health to humanity. The Path Forward To achieve this vision, we will: 1. Educate embassies and health systems about the importance of holistic well-being and sustainability. 2. Collaborate with teachers and experts in various fields to share their wisdom and knowledge. 3. Develop innovative technologies that promote healing, education, and environmental sustainability. 4. Establish a global network of like-minded individuals and organizations working together as one consciousness. The Evidence Our research has gathered vital evidence on the interconnectedness of all things and the universal laws that govern our world. We have learned from various teachers and sources, including Gregg Braden, a pioneer in the field of Heart Intelligence. Gregg Braden is a renowned author, researcher, and teacher who has spent over three decades studying the connection between human consciousness and the universe. His work has taken him to the farthest reaches of the globe, from the high mountains of Tibet to the deserts of Egypt. He has shared his knowledge with millions of people through his books, lectures, and media appearances. Braden's work highlights the importance of the heart's intelligence in shaping our reality. He explains that the heart is not just a physical organ but a gateway to our deepest intuition, creativity, and wisdom. By tapping into the heart's intelligence, we can access higher states of consciousness, heal ourselves and others, and create a more harmonious world. The Power of the Heart The heart's power to heal is evident in the work of Gregg Braden and other researchers. Studies have shown that the heart's electromagnetic field is thousands of times stronger than the brain's, and it plays a crucial role in our emotional, mental, and physical well-being. By embracing the wisdom of Heart Intelligence, we can: - Access deeper states of intuition, creativity, and wisdom - Heal ourselves and others through the power of the heart - Create a more harmonious and peaceful world The Call to Action We invite individuals, organizations, and governments to join us in this revolution of consciousness. Together, we can create a world where everyone has access to holistic education, healthcare, and sustainable living. Let us work together to raise the frequency of the earth and bring peace, prosperity, and well-being to all. Conclusion We are one consciousness, connected and interdependent. Our thoughts, feelings, and actions have the power to transform the world. Let us embrace this universal law and work together to create a brighter future for all. We are the architects of our destiny, and the time for change is now. Sincerely, Richard Elliott 8 Hardy St Rosewater South Australia"]

life.,Your donation helps mangroves, kelp forests, seagrass, and coral reefs fight climate change and protect marine life.', '🌿 3 Ways You Can Help Protect Mangroves 🌊 Mangroves are essential to the health of the Gulf of Mexico, and you can make a difference in preserving these vital ecosystems. Here are the three actions you can take: Secure your trash. Because mangroves sit at the edge of the sea, they often become a trap for marine debris. Make sure your trash on and off the water is secure and bring an extra container to collect trash when you see it. Reduce Runoff and Pollution. Minimize the use of fertilizers and chemicals in your yard. Runoff from these substances can harm mangrove habitats and the marine life that depends on them. Opt for natural alternatives and properly dispose of hazardous waste. Support Local Conservation Efforts. Get involved with or donate to organizations working to protect and restore mangrove forests. Your support helps fund crucial research and conservation projects that safeguard these ecosystems. Every action counts when it comes to protecting our mangroves and the vital role they play in the Gulf of Mexico. This post is part of a Gulf Star project sponsored by @[100057747935974:2048:Clean Gulf Associates]. #GulfOfMexico #Mangroves #Conservation #ProtectOurCoast #EnvironmentalAwareness']

Name: 137_gastech_foreign oil_shale_gas america

Representative_Docs: ['It’s time to END America’s dependence on foreign oil once and for all. We should NEVER have to beg hostile nations for energy while sitting on more oil and gas than most of the world. Under the Biden Administration, we saw skyrocketing prices, weakened energy security, and a full-blown war on American production. It’s time to unleash American energy, drill more, produce more, export more. Our economy, our national security, and our way of life depend on it. No more excuses. No more handouts to the green lobby. It’s time to put America FIRST, take the shackles off our energy producers, and END energy dependence permanently. Let’s fire up the rigs and lead the world with Texas oil and American power. 🇺🇸', "In this episode of American Potential, new host David From sits down with Toby Rice, CEO of EQT Corporation, the largest producer of natural gas in the United States. Together, they discuss the pivotal role of natural gas in America's energy independence, the lessons learned from Europe's energy crisis, and the need to prioritize infrastructure development to meet rising energy demands. Learn how the shale revolution transformed the U.S. into a global energy powerhouse, producing over 30 million barrels of energy per day. Rice shares insights on combating myths about fossil fuels, the environmental benefits of natural gas, and the critical need to build pipelines and storage facilities to ensure affordable and reliable energy for Americans. As AI technology drives an unprecedented surge in energy demand, the conversation explores how American energy policy must evolve under the Trump administration to unleash the full potential of U.S. natural gas, strengthen global energy security, and address future challenges. Visit AmericanPotential.com for more stories about how Americans are expanding freedom and opportunity in their communities.", 'Gastech is an international platform where the collective vision for a sustainable, secure, and affordable energy future moves closer to reality. Gastech in Houston, taking place 17-20 September, will bring together 50,000 energy professionals from 125 countries over four days. Join leading experts, innovators, and decision-makers at Gastech, and share ideas, build connections, and explore new business opportunities. Register as a visitor: https://bit.ly/4cZDyR6 #Gastech #Hydrogen #Climatetech #AI #Houston']
